[px]FOREWORD 
[px]E.O.D.H., the  publishing  arm  of  Bethel  (The  House  Of  God), her  associate  ministers  and  churches  of  the  Caribbean, South  America,  United  States, 
Germany, India, Africa, Philippines and other countries, are pleased to present to you  E.O.D.H. Book 15 entitled, ―Pentecostalism, 100 years, Versus The Final 
outpouring of the Holy Ghost.  
[px] This  work is based upon sermons preached by pastor Dalton Bruce of Bethel, Trinidad and pastors of  Grace  Covenant  Church, K. Mcgahee, and  G. 
Renner of Middletown Conn. U.S.A., in July and August 2006. Because of the urgency to have this subject published at this time publication of the faith of Esau, 
Judas  and  Simon  the  Sorcerer  will  appear  on  a  following  edition,  though  it  was  promised  to  be  included  in  E.O.D.H.  book 15,  we  apologize  for  any 
inconvenience to our subscribers.   
[px] We trust that this work would be a blessing to our associates and message believers internationally, religious people and others around the world.  
[px] These messages, based  upon  the  Holy  Ghost outpouring  of  1906  and  beyond, were  of  tremendous  edification  to  all  ministers  and  saints,  who  heard 
them personally by audio and also by video through internet medium, DVDs and other electronic facilities.  
[px] We  trust that this  work  will be  of edification to  you and  all those who would read it.  Our sincere  efforts are  to enlighten all our readers to the  final 
outpouring of the Holy Ghost. Our purpose is not to present to message believers an eighth messenger or any of our ministers, including the authors of E.O.D.H., 
as prophets, revealers of the seven thunders or any new revelation.  
[px]However, we  humbly  confess  that  the  message  of Malachi  4:5-6  and  the  written  Word  of  God, is  the  absolute  for  the Bride  of  Christ, and  all who 
preach  contrary  to  them are  wrong, and  all  teachings  that  contradicts  the  Word  and  revelation  of  the  prophet  are  heresies.  Thus  the  title  of  our  publication: 
―Exposition Of Damnable Heresies‖ –E.O.D.H.- Amen.  
[px]Preached,  written  and  published  By  the  Author: Pastor  Dalton  Bruce - Bethel  {The  House  Of  God},  Co-Authors: Kenneth 
Mcgahee & Gerald Renner - Grace Covenant Church Conn. U.S.A.,  in August 2006. 
[px]INTRODUCTION 
[px] Forasmuch as many churches, millions of Pentecostals by organization and followers of the  message of Malachi 4:5&6 bring into remembrance and 
commemorate 100 years of ―Pentecostalism‖, it seems good to us to be a part of this commemoration. 
[px] We, message  believers  and  ministers  from  the  Caribbean,  South  America,  North  America,  India,  Africa,  Germany,  Philippines  and  other  countries 
send greetings to all who rejoice at this memorable occasion of 100 years of ―Pentecostalism‖, which officially started in 1906 at 312 Azuza St., Los Angeles, 
California, U.S.A.   
[px] Special  greetings  to  the  followers  of  the  gospel  message,  of  W.M. Branham,  who  have  deputed  for  the  prophet  in  this  commemoration  of  the 
outpouring of the baptism of the Holy Ghost and revival of 1906. He had the grand privilege in 1956 to join the celebration of the Azuza jubilee: 50 years of the 
outpouring of the  Holy Ghost. He  entitled his  message, ―Azuza Jubilee‖. The  church has arrived at  the  second Azuza jubilee;  the  final  outpouring of the  Holy 
Spirit is at hand.  
[px] E.O.D.H., the publishing arm of Bethel (The House Of God), her associate ministers and churches internationally, embrace this wonderful opportunity, 
on this occasion, to be a part of this commemoration with Pentecostals by organization and the followers of Malachi 4:5-6 in presenting to you and the rest of the 
world E.O.D.H book 15 entitled, ―Pentecostalism - 100 years, Versus Pentecost. The Final outpouring of the Holy Ghost‖.  
[px] We deem this work most important to the followers of the message, and the religious world, as the church makes ready for the final outpouring of the 
Holy  Spirit  for  rapture  power.  In  our  documentation  we  fully  recognized  the  1906 revival  as  a  great  move  of  God,  and  the  founding  fathers  and  the  great 
revivalists, who followed with many outpourings and revivals, to be of God. We confirmed the same by the written Word of God, the history of ―Pentecostalism‖ 
and the message of Malachi 4:5-6.  
[px] Nevertheless, we told the truth from historical facts. The highlight of this work is to prove that the 1906 outpouring and revival was a forerunner to the 
final outpouring of the Holy Spirit for rapture power. It was not the literal fulfillment of Joel 2:23-28 and a repeat of Acts 2:4. The same was confirmed by the 
prophet‘s message (Church Ages 377-380). 
[px]PENTECOSTALISM - 100 YEARS 
 THE CREAM OF THE CROP 
[px]CHAPTER  1  
[px] At the turn of the 20
th
 century, Pentecostalism was born an infant church by the baptism of the Holy Ghost in 1906. One hundred years have elapsed 
since God sent that revival at 312 Azusa St., Los Angeles, California U.S.A. When God sent that revival, it shook the world, and Los Angeles, the city of angels, 
became  very popular, around  the  world. However, they  were  not  without persecutions from the  rest of the  unbelieving  world. They  were  despised, hated and 
rejected, both by religious and non-religious people. Nevertheless, she waxed stronger and stronger each day. In one hundred years, she has grown into one of the 
greatest religious powers, and now ranks with the greater denominational churches, established before her: Roman Catholics, Anglicans, Lutherans, Methodist, 
Baptist etc.  
[px] 19,000, 000: ―Estimated numbers of Pentecostals vary widely. Christianity Today reported in an article titled World Growth at 19 Million a Year.‖ 
“25 percent of the world's Christians are Pentecostal or charismatic.‖ 
400 – 600 million:  ―The highest estimates place the figure between 400 and 600 million.‖ 
11,000: “There are about 11,000 different pentecostal or charismatic denominations worldwide. Pentecostalism is sometimes referred to as the "third 
force of Christianity.‖  
780,000: ―The largest Christian church in the world is the Yoido Full Gospel Church in South Korea, a Pentecostal church. Founded and led by David  
(Paul) Yonggi Cho since 1958, it had 780,000 members in 2003. Chinese believers on the mainland headquarters are now in Taiwan. The Apostolic Church is 
the fastest growing church in the world. Pentecostalism is the world's fastest-growing Christian movement.‖  Source of info.: (Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia) 
[px]MILLIONS OF SOULS SAVED 
[px]Pentecostalism, from her infancy, was used of God to usher millions of souls into the kingdom of God. Revivals after revivals were born, outpouring 
after outpouring of the Holy Spirit showered from Heaven. Great revivalists and servants of God arose from that mighty move of God. One of the greatest  
revivals was born in 1946. It shook the world by mighty servants of God, gifts  
of healing, and mighty signs and wonders.   
[px]Early  Leaders  are: Maria  Woodworth-Etter,  Smith  Wigglesworth,  Charles  Fox  Parham,  William  J.  Seymour, Bishop  Johnson,  William  Sowders, 
George  Jeffreys,  Aimee  Semple  McPherson,  David  du  Plessis,  Kathryn  Kuhlman, Jack  Coe,  A.  A.  Allen,  Oral  Roberts,  Rex  Humbard  the  first  successful  TV 
evangelist of the mid 1950s, 1960s, and the 1970s and at one time had the largest television audience of any televangelist in the U.S.  
[px]The 1946/47 international  revival  which originated  with the  renowned evangelist,  W. M. Branham and lasted the  longest ever in history ended in the 
early sixties. The flames of fire slowly went out from 1962. That event paved the way for the fulfillment of many Bible prophecies.  
Pentecostalism  was ordained  of God to fulfill  some  of the  greatest prophecies  in the  end time.  She  has spread her  wings to the  cardinal points of the 
globe, in might and power, wealth and riches, popularity and fame. Her greatness is too enormous to reiterate. Paper and ink are insufficient to document all her 
[px]achievements  and  successes  for  the  pass  one  hundred  years.  Nevertheless,  certain  important  factors  must  be  documented  in  this work  to  enlighten  the  world 
concerning her tremendous part in the fulfillment of Bible prophecies.  
[px]WORD OF FAITH REVIVAL- MODERN PENTECOSTALISM 
[px]Another mighty Pentecostal revival arose only a few years after the revival fires of 1946/47 went out. It was termed the ―third wave‖, 1906 being the 
first wave and 1946/47 the second wave. The father of this modern movement was Mr. Kenneth Hagin and E.W. Kenyons 
[px]Quote: It was known as ―Word of Faith, also known as Word-Faith or simply Faith. 
 It is  a  movement  within Pentecostal and charismatic churches  worldwide.  Its  central  doctrine  is  that  health  and  prosperity  are  promised  to  all 
believers, and are available through faith.‖ ―Other names, reflecting major aspects of the movement, include "Name it and claim it", "The Prosperity Gospel", 
and "Positive Confession". 
―Word-Faith teachings trace their roots to E. W. Kenyon (1867-1948),  
a New England evangelical pastor who taught that health and finances were the  
right  of  every  believer  who  would  claim  the  promises  of Scripture through  faith.  Kenyon  claimed  God's  promises  were  realized  by  our  believing  and  verbally 
confessing the relevant scriptures, and thus he coined the phrase, "What I confess, I possess." 
―Pentecostal preacher Kenneth Hagin (1917-2003), of Tulsa  
Oklahoma, was heavily  influenced by Kenyon's writings, and began teaching the  same doctrines as early  as the  1930s. Hagin is often referred to as being the 
"father" of the modern Word-Faith movement. He elaborated on Kenyon's theology of confession, preaching a four-part formula for receiving God's promises: 
"Say it; do it; receive it; tell it." 
―Other preachers in Tulsa were mentored by "Dad" Hagin, and began to preach the same doctrines. The most prominent of these is Kenneth Copeland. 
“Proponents of the doctrine in the United States include Creflo Dollar, Frederick K. C. Price, Benny Hinn, Joel Osteen, Marilyn Hickey, Rod Parsley, 
Jesse  Duplantis, Oral  Roberts, Joyce  Meyer, John  Avanzini and Keith  Butler,  among  others. Many  of  these  pastors  appear  regularly  on Trinity  Broadcasting 
Network, whose founders and directors Paul and Jan Crouch are also proponents of the Word-Faith doctrine. 
―In Australia, Pastor Brian Houston of Hillsong Church and Evangelist Tim Hall promote Word-Faith doctrine. In South Africa, Ray McCauley is the 
movement's   chief   proponent,   and   in   the United   Kingdom, Clive   Pick and Paul   Scanlon are   among   the   main   Word-Faith   preachers.   Quote: 
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Word-of-Faith.  
[px]SUCCESS OF MODERN PENTECOSTALISM 
MR. HAGIN – COPELAND - CROUCH 
[px] Pentecostalism, by ―word of faith‖ movement, and dad Hagin, K. Copeland and Paul Crouch greatly inspired many evangelists. It removed them from 
the old-fashioned gospel tent meetings, to the radio and television network. Hundreds of millions of souls could now be reached in their very homes. The Trinity 
Broadcasting  Network  is  the  greatest  media  of  them  all.  The  third  wave  movement  grew  and  prospered  by  leaps  and  bounds  in  such  a  manner  that  seems 
incredible. However, Bible  prophecies are  being  fulfilled by the  instrumentality of Pentecostalism. This  will be  discussed in later chapters of this book. She  is 
hailed as the third force of Christianity.  
[px] Pentecostalism, by  the  third  wave,  became  a  mighty  force,  insomuch  that  her  influence  reached  into  other  Protestant  denominations  and  the  Roman 
Catholic Church. Priests and church members confessed that they were receiving the baptism of the Holy Ghost. They speak in tongues and experience the gifts 
of  the  Spirit,  with  divine  healing.  From  this  great  achievement  the  catholic  charismatic  movement  was  born  in  Pennsylvania  USA.  Statistics  of  2003  claims  a 
membership of 119 million in 238 countries. Oral Roberts is one of the great leaders of that movement.  
[px]PROSPERITY REVIVAL 
[px] The  Word  of  faith  movement  laid  a  solid  foundation  for  preaching  healing  and  prosperity  of  most  preachers  of  Pentecostalism.  Most  of  them  are 
millionaires and several are billionaires. Many preachers were influenced and received their qualifications from Oral Roberts University. K. Copeland and Hagin 
himself fall into this category.  
[px]―Hagin never received any formal theological training of any kind. His doctorate was honorarily given by Oral Roberts University in the 1970s.  Hagin 
was considered by his followers to be a dynamic preacher, teacher, and prophet, known for preaching healing and prosperity through searching God‘s word and 
believing God in faith for financial gifts, then claiming and receiving them by faith...‖  ―However,  walking  in  love  was  a  priority  message  throughout  his 
ministry.   
―Since its inception, his organization, Kenneth  Hagin  Ministries,  grew  to  include numerous media outreaches  and  ministries.    These  include  Faith 
Library Publications, with 65 million book copies in print; RHEMA Praise‖, a weekly television program on the Trinity Broadcasting Network; ―Faith Seminar 
of the Air‖, a radio program heard on many stations nationwide and on the Internet; ―The Word of Faith‖, a free monthly  magazine  with  roughly  250,000 
subscribers;  crusades  conducted  throughout  the  nation;  RHEMA  Correspondence  Bible  School;  and  the  RHEMA  Prayer  and  Healing  Center.‖ Quote: 
http://en.wikipedia.org/. 
[px]KENNETH COPELAND 
[px]―Kenneth Copeland (born December 6, 1936 in Lubbock, TX) is a Pentecostal Word of Faith preacher and televangelist.‖ 
―Kenneth Copeland claimed to be a successful pop singer before turning his life over to ministry work following his conversion on November 2, 1962. 
In the 1960s, he was a pilot for faith healer Oral Roberts. Copeland attended Oral Roberts University in Tulsa, Oklahoma, but never graduated. Later he studied 
under  another  Pentecostal  evangelist, Kenneth  E.  Hagin.  His  ministry  grew  through  the 1970s, and in 1979, ―Believer‘s Voice  of  Victory‖ first appeared on 
television.‖ 
―Kenneth Copeland Ministries has its headquarters in Fort Worth, Texas. Since 1991, Copeland and his wife, Gloria, have been holding annual week-
long ―Believers‘ Conventions‖ in both California and Texas. His recent regular preaching partners at these events have been Creflo A. Dollar, Jesse Duplantis, 
and Jerry Savelle.‖ 
―He is considered one of the founding members of the Word of Faith  
movement.‖ 
―Copeland‟s teachings incorporate both ―Classical Pentecostalism‖, with its emphasis on the work of the Holy Spirit, including speaking in tongues, as 
well as elements of Faith theology. 
[px]―He teaches that prosperity and riches are promised to every believer, and can be gained by faith. Faith is a ―force‖, activated by ―confession‖, i.e. 
speaking the words of God.‖ Quote: http://en.wikipedia.org/. 
[px] Both Hagin and Copeland inspired thousands by the Holy laughter and drunkenness revival. More on that supernatural move later in this chapter.  
[px]TRINITY BROADCASTING NETWORK – TBN – PAUL CROUCH 
[px] A way was supernaturally created for the Word of faith movement to circle the Globe. It paved the way for millions to share in that great revival. It is 
called TBN. 
“Paul Franklin Crouch (born March  29, 1934)  is  the  co-founder,  chairman,  and  president  of  the Trinity  Broadcasting  Network (TBN),  the  world's

TH
 YEAR OF PENTECOSTALISM -

[px]Around  the  world,  in  this  2006,there  are  many  celebrations.  They  are  celebrating  the  event  of  one  hundred  years  of  Pentecostalism,  which  started  in 
1906. And the celebrations have stretched far and wide concerning this great event that took place in 1906, which, I believe, was the hand of the  Lord in 1906 
[px]that  sent the showers of blessings to the  people  of God.  There  is  something involved here  which I  want to bring to  your attention and to the  attention of the 
world.  
Followers of the message around the world are also involved in celebrating this great event of one hundred years of Pentecostalism where Pentecost was 
poured out, the Baptism of the Holy Spirit, in 1906.  And we want to take the time to examine this great claim of Pentecost returning in 1906. I want to do this in 
a Scriptural  manner that a  fool shall not err therein, that  when we  have come to the end there should be no doubts and there should be  no fear. Credit must be 
given to the people of 1906 all the same, because I believe that it was a great blessing of the Lord.  
You might not understand the term, ―Pentecostalism,‖ but there is a great body of people upon the earth right now for one hundred years. Their system 
has been drawn up. And we refer to them as Pentecostals. Now Pentecostals are just not one body of people all united, but I will venture to say that there are 
hundreds, if not thousands, of different organizations around the world known by different titles, but they all relate back to 1906. They were a hated, despised and 
rejected people once, but today they are very popular. Their preachers are, around the world, very well recognized by the governments. They are popular on the 
television, and whatever media that they use. We have great men. And great men preached and lived for the past one hundred years, so you can imagine the great 
celebrations that they are having.   
One of our brothers visited a celebration in Germany and he gave  me reports of the great time that they are  having; people gathering from all parts of 
Europe to celebrate one hundred years of Pentecostalism. And they believe with all their hearts that it was the return of Pentecost.   
Now,  why  this  is  so important to  us  and  to  the  people  who  follow  this  message,  and  I  will  venture  to  say,  to  all  religious  people,  is  that there  are 
millions of people today who rely upon this blessing of 1906 to prepare them for the Rapture. And the message believers are included, right there. Better than 
ninety-nine percent of the people that follow the message are based upon that experience, that Outpouring of the Baptism of the Holy Spirit and that revival that 
broke in 1906. All that they have added to the experience of 1906 is the message of God that was brought to us by the Laodicean messenger. That is all that they 
added; more truths. But the truth is that they rely upon that experience of 1906 and they think that they are better off than the Pentecostals because they received 
a message from God and a prophet of God. And they think that they are all qualified and prepared for the Rapture and, any time the Rapture can take place.   
So  I  want  to examine such  claims  in  the  Scripture  this  morning.  We  want  to  continue  to  examine  such  claims  tomorrow  night,  Tuesday  night,  and 
Wednesday night. This is going to be a great lesson for the church and the ministers, and it will long live on, and I‘m trusting and hoping for great deliverance 
when these messages go out, because it will be irrefutable. It will be undeniable, except for the unbelief of the people—and unbelief does not hinder God.   
[px]Quote W.M.B.: 70 Unbelief doesn't stop God; it doesn't hinder Him. I don't care how much the world unbelieves, It's still going to happen. Unbelief 
doesn't do nothing but condemns the unbeliever. The unbelief will send the unbeliever to hell. It'll rob him of every blessing God promised to him, but it doesn't

[px]The name ―Pentecostal‖ came from the Scripture read in Acts 2. That is why I read that Scripture. Because in 1906 the people believed that shower of 
blessing that came  upon them was actually a return of Pentecost, that the same thing that took place on the day of Pentecost took place in 1906. As a result of 
that, the name was given to that system and I would say ―organization‖ because they organized soon after and entitled that body of people, ―Pentecostals.‖

[px]“RETURN OF PENTECOST IN 1906”, THE GREATEST LIE 
[px]One  of the greatest event that  will ever take  place  in this  twenty-first century and in  human history is another Outpouring of the Holy  Spirit for the 
end-time Church, like the Day of Pentecost. ―Why do you say that preacher?‖ Even because the rapture is dependent upon this Outpouring of the Holy Spirit. 
Another revival coming  to the  end-time  Church is dependent upon another Outpouring of the  Spirit.  The translation and rapture of the  Church is dependent 
upon this Outpouring of the Holy Spirit. And the dead that died in Christ since the time of our Lord Jesus are dependent upon this great Outpouring of the Holy 
Spirit.   
Since  this  Outpouring  of  the  Holy  Spirit  is  one  of  the  greatest  events  that  will  ever  take place  in  human  history,  to  make  a  claim  that  that  Power  did 
return to the Church in 1906, is the greatest lie that was ever told in the twentieth century. Any time that you tell such a lie about the greatest event for the Church 
of God, you are trying to take away the greatest Promise that God ever made to the end time Church. So it is the greatest lie that was ever told in the twentieth 
century.   
Now, think of how  many lies  were  told in the twentieth century—religious lies, political lies and lies of  mankind. Can  you estimate  it? Mountains  of 
lies; oceans of lies. I do not think that, if you would empty the waters from the sea, that there would be space enough for the lies of man that he told just for the 
twentieth century. We were born liars. But thank God for Jesus Christ, that we can serve Him today in Spirit and truth. Think of the lies. Men lie from morning to 
night. And they even lie in their dreams. Such is the human nature. But I am saying that out of all the oceans of lies that were told in the twentieth century, this is 
the greatest lie: that Pentecost had indeed returned in 1906, and by the Grace of God, I can prove that in the Scriptures.   
All right! Since this was adopted from Acts 2 and they claim that it was a repeat of Pentecost, I go back to Acts 2. Now here today we have thousands of 
organizations  and  millions  of  churches,  all  in  the  Name  of  Jesus  Christ.  But,  according  to  Scripture,  Mathew  16:18,  Jesus  only  spoke  about  one  Church,  a

Matthew 16:18 <...upon this rock I will build my church...> (Singular!)   
Even in the days of our Lord Jesus, the Church was not yet built, but He was speaking about building a Church in the future—one single Church. And 
that is what we believe that we are in the making today. That is why we are not an organization; we are not a denomination and we are not a religion. We believe 
that  we  will  be  part  of  this  Church  of  Jesus  Christ.  And  this  Church  in  Acts  2  was  inaugurated  on  the  day  of  Pentecost.  The  prophecy  that  our  Lord  made  in 
Mathew 16:18 about building a Church, a singular Church, happened on the day of Pentecost. And how did that happen? It happened by an Outpouring of the 
Spirit that came in such a measure that it put the believers into the mystical Body of our Lord Jesus Christ. Not the body, the human body called Jesus Christ, but 
the mystical Body. It immersed them into a certain realm, which is called the Kingdom of God. And then by that Outpouring of the Holy Spirit putting the souls 
of the believers into that mystical realm of the Spirit, they became the Church of God. And there was no need for a roll book. There was no need for a name on 
their church building, because it was the Church of our Lord Jesus Christ. One Church.   
Then that one Church, according to Ephesians 4, had one Lord—not three gods, one faith, and one baptism.  
Ephesians 4:5 <One Lord, one faith, one baptism...> 
That is how the early Church functioned. They were a united people. According to Acts, they were in one heart, and one accord.  
Acts  2:46  <And  they,  continuing  daily  with  one  accord  in  the  temple,  and  breaking  bread  from  house  to house, did  eat  their  meat  with  gladness  and

They were a united bunch of people, and they had one Lord, one faith, and one baptism. Then they had one Bible. But today we have many faiths. You

[px]Reformation period – article of Pentecost missing  
[px]All right! Let us look at the word ―reformation.‖ It means to reform. Now the only  reason  that  you  will  want  to  reform  something  is  because  it  was 
destroyed, and you are trying to bring it back to its original state or condition. So there started a reformation. Luther came out of the Catholic Church. He was a 
monk, a priest, and he found a Bible in the dungeons or the secret places of the Pope. He brought that out and then he started a reformation in his time and started 
to reform the church toward Pentecost—toward the original. So he brought to the public that you do not have to confess to the priest. You do not have to find any 
―holy father‖ to forgive you for your sins. You can go directly to the Lord Jesus Christ, confess your sins and be forgiven.  
[px]So now Martin Luther came with the message of ―Justification by faith,‖ and it caused a world revival, even without power, signs, and wonders. And 
they were called ―Protestants.‖ The reason that they were called ―Protestants‖ is that they protested the greatest church upon earth, called the  
Universal Church. And they were tagged ―Protestants.‖ 
Now, I want you to understand that that great Article that came on the day of Pentecost was not amongst the people in the Dark Ages.  That is proven in 
chapter 6 of the book of Revelation. They had just a little anointing and they had just a little bit of the Word insomuch that the Lamb, Jesus Christ, had to cry out 
and say,  
Revelation 6:6 <...hurt not the oil and the wine. > 
 If the Lord had not intervened in the Dark Ages, every speck of the Holy Spirit would have left the church, and every speck of the Word would have 
been lost from the Church of God. But they survived with a little anointing of the Spirit and a little bit of the Word of God.   
[px]Quote  W.M.B.: 216-2  All  through  the  Dark  Ages  she  has  dominated.  For  over  nine  hundred  years  she  plundered  and  destroyed. She  killed  the  arts, 
destroyed the sciences, produced nothing but death until the light of the Truth was almost entirely gone and only the barest sliver of light remained. The oil and 
the wine had almost ceased to flow. (Thyatirean Church Age - Church Age Book Cpt.6). 
[px]Quote: 170-3 If the church, way back there in that third age had only held on to the revelation of the living Word in their midst, the power of God would 
not have faded as it did in those Dark Ages. (Pergamean Church Age -  Church Age Book Cpt.5). 
[px]Now  when  Martin  Luther  came  (I  want  to  stress  right  here),  although  he  had  a  great  revival  that  struck  the  world  out  of  Germany,  there  was no 
Outpouring of the Holy Spirit like the day of Pentecost, neither in Germany nor with Martin Luther. It was still on its way to come. I hope you catch the trend of 
my  thinking  now.  But  that  revival  of  Martin  Luther  shook  the  world  without  power,  signs,  and  wonders.  The  reason  that  the  power,  signs,  and  wonders  were 
lacking is that what they had on the day of Pentecost had not returned. Jesus said,  
Acts  1:8  <But  ye  shall  receive  power,  after  that  the  Holy  Ghost  is  come  upon  you:  and  ye  shall  be  witnesses  unto  me  both  in  Jerusalem,  and  in  all 
Judaea, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth. > 
[px]MISSED BAPTISM  
[px]Quote W.M.B.: 257-3 I wish it could be said that the Sardisean Age was a restoration instead of a reformation. I cannot say that. The Word does not 
call it a restoration, but certainly calls it a reformation. Had it been a restoration, that age would have been another Pentecostal age. But it was not. The best 
that  could  be  said  of  it  was,  "Strengthen  that  which  remains,  that  is  ready  to  die."  There  was  something missing.  My,  yes,  there  certainly  was.  This  age  had 
justification, but it had missed sanctification and the Baptism with the Holy Ghost... The same Spirit that was in Jesus while He was here on earth, causing Him 
to  do  the  mighty  works  which  He  did  came  back  upon  the  church  at  Pentecost  so  that  they  did  the  works  that He  did.  This  age  did  not  have  those  works. 
(Sardisean Church Age - Church Age Book Cpt.7). 
[px]HOLY GHOST BOUND IN THE CHURCH AGES FOR 2000 YEARS 
[px]  Quote: 29-6 ...that church has bound the Holy Ghost for nearly two thousand years, under martyrdom back there and under the church ages. It's been 
bound, not at the river Euphrates, but at the door of creeds and dogmas, that the Holy Spirit can't work in the church because of manmade systems. But She's 
going to be liberated. (Feast Of The Trumpets 64-0719m). 
[px]So now that was one of the proofs that whatever they had on the day of Pentecost did not come back with Martin Luther. Luther went on and when he 
died they formed an organization. 
[px]A little after there arose another man in England by the name of John  
Wesley. John Wesley did a little better than Luther and said that you must live a sanctified life. God anointed John Wesley. He gave him a great ministry, even 
with some signs and wonders. But I stress again here, that in the days of John Wesley, Pentecost had not returned. The great Outpouring of the Holy Spirit had 
not  yet  come  although a Restoration was promised in the  book of Joel. The  Restoration of Power  where  God  will pour out His  Spirit upon all  flesh, had not 
returned. So now the Church was still reforming.   
Here is a building. It got broken down and you are cleaning up the spot, trying to establish a foundation. You are gathering lumber; you are gathering 
bricks; you are reforming. But the building is not yet restored. And here you lay a little foundation and put up a few bricks but the building is not restored. And 
then another builder passes by and he puts up some walls and fixes certain things in there, but the building is not yet restored. So we call that reformation. When 
the building gets the capstone and everything is fixed, and the paint goes on the walls and all those other things, then the building is fully restored. Everybody is 
going to know that this is the building that represents the building that was demolished—by a storm, by a hurricane or by an earthquake. They will realize that 
this building is restored. It looks just like that one that the earthquake pushed down.   
Now, based on the prophecy of Joel 2:23-26, great men out of the Lutherans and the Methodists knew that that Scripture existed there. They knew that 
there was a Promise of Restoration. As certainly, as you and I today are familiar with these Scriptures that there will be a Restoration of a Church like the day of 
Pentecost, there were men that read those same Scriptures. So these men reading those great Scriptures in that time were Methodists—desperate men—and they 
longed for this Restoration. And they knew that Restoration has to do with another return of Pentecost. Because when Restoration is spoken of in the book of 
Joel, chapter 2, verses 23 right down to 26, it comes right down and start talking about ―I will pour out my Spirit.‖ Spiritual men in the Methodist church had two 
great things in mind; the pouring out of the latter rain, and they knew very well that that is translated to mean the outpouring of the Holy Spirit. So now this is 
what is going to bring in, Pentecostalism of 1906.  
We have established here that reformation is not Restoration. Reformation is different from Restoration. Restoration is the complete thing. So here we 
had great men. And I am going to tell you about some of them. And I will let you into the facts of the fathers of Pentecostalism. Not ―fathers of Pentecost,‖ but 
―fathers of Pentecostalism.‖ I am going to compare in a little  while  what these fathers received at 1906 and I will compare them  with the  fathers of the day of 
Pentecost. I believe we should have a clear picture after that. 
[px]LACKED PLAN BACK TO Pentecost – CLAIMS EXAMINED 
[px]So they sought the return of Pentecost on the basis of Joel 2: 23-26 and they knew that would come by the Latter Rain, outpouring of the Spirit. Now 
one  thing that  is important  here:  they did not  have enough  knowledge  of  the plan of God or the program of God to  have  this return of Pentecost.  They  went 
according to their own  knowledge, their own  understanding; they did their best.  They  were  great  men; they  were  wonderful  men, even like  Martin  Luther and 
John Wesley. Great men, they were sincere men.   
Now Luther was a Trinitarian, believing in three gods and he was even involved in the catechism from the Catholic church, showing you how dark the 
hour  was.  Wesley  took some  of the  Catholic doctrine  and he  brought it over too. These men coming out of the  Catholic, Lutheran and Methodist church  were 
Trinitarians. The revelation of ―one God‖ had not yet come to them. (Think of how dark that hour was.) They were Trinitarians, believing that  God  was three 
gods! A Father, a Son and the Holy Ghost. You pray to the Father a little bit, then you pray to the Son a little bit, then you pray to the Holy Ghost a little bit, and 
if you feel that the Father is jealous then you come back to the Father and start worshiping Him. It becomes confusing to the mind.   
Now we believe in Father, Son, and Holy Ghost but we do not believe that they are three gods. We believe in one God (Saint John 4:24) and we believe 
that God was manifested in three different offices or attributes. 
John 4:24 <God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.> 
[px] In the Old Testament, He was Father of Israel. He took a bodily form and came down on the earth and He was the Son. Through His death, burial and 
resurrection, He came back on the day of Pentecost as the Holy Ghost—one God. 
So these were great men, and (I want to stress here that in 1906,) they did receive a genuine experience of the Holy Ghost. I will even venture to call it 
(like the prophet called it) the baptism of the Holy Ghost. So they did receive the baptism of the Holy Ghost in 1906. They received an outpouring in 1906 and 
they received the gifts of the Spirit.  
[px]BAPTISM OF THE HOLY GHOST IN THE LAST DAYS 
[px]Quote W.M.B.: 116 That's what the church did. It entered into justification under Luther. Then it went into sanctification under Wesley, went into the 
baptism  of  the  Holy  Ghost  in  this  last  days,  and  now  resting  with  the  Earnest  of  our  inheritance  that  we  know  that  something happened  in  us; (Revelation 
Chapter Five 2  61-0618). 
[px]Quote: 127-2 This last age  started with the  fires of a Pentecostal revival and they organized it right back. Instead of taking the Word they took  their 
own  ideas  and  did  just  what  every  age  has  done--substituted the manual for the Word...They are second generation Pentecostals and since God  doesn't  have 
grandchildren they are just children of their fathers, known by their creeds and form of worship. They can talk about what used to be, but they can't produce it. 
Once they had the lightning but about all that is left is the thunder. But let them talk to you about the glories of their movement. They will say, "Yes sir, I want 
you to know that this is a move that no man started. It came spontaneously. The Spirit fell all over the world. Yes sir, we got what they had at Pentecost. This was 
not of men but of God." THEN WHY DIDN'T THEY KEEP IT THAT WAY? IF GOD STARTED IT HOW COME GOD COULDN'T KEEP IT AND FINISH IT? If 
God didn't  write a handbook of creeds and formulas and dogmas to start it,  then what right did they have to do that? (Smyrnaean  Church  Age - Church  Age 
Book Cpt.). 
[px]They claimed that it was a return and a repeat of Pentecost. Now we are to examine these claims. That is why we are gathered here today and will be 
gathered for a few days. We want to examine by the Scripture the claims of 1906 and these great men.   
[px]Quote W.M.B.: 96-1 Where, oh where, are we spiritually? We are in a wilderness of darkness. How far we have wandered from the first church.  
Pentecost is nowhere in view and the Word cannot be found. (Ephesian Church Age  -  Church Age Book Cpt.3). 
[px]They majored on the gifts of the Spirit. They read in the Bible where men did signs and wonders and miracles.  They read the Book of Acts and they 
looked back in chapter 2. They were smart enough to realize that this had an origin. All that was written in the Book of Acts had an origin and that is, it started on 
the day of Pentecost. Then the  miracles, signs and wonders went on throughout the Book of Acts and they knew that they had to start at the beginning so they 
were gathered together to ask God for another return of Pentecost but they had in their minds above all, ―gifts of the Spirit.‖ They majored on gifts of the Spirit. 
They wanted to do the things like they read about in the Book of Acts. (And we want to do them to, but you have to start the right  
way.)  
God has a plan; He has a program; He has keys to this thing. They were ignorant of God‘s plan and program of how to get back to Pentecost. So they 
did their very best and we appreciate that. We are not condemning those great fathers of Pentecost of 1906. We are not saying that it was of the devil. We are not 
saying that they were hypocrites or not sincere in what they were doing and that their desires were not right.   
I want everybody to understand that I am not saying that what happened at 1906 was not of God. I believe that what happened in 1906 was of God.  I 
believe it was the  genuine Holy Ghost. I believe the  men that sought God were sincere  men. They were great men who loved the  Lord.  I believe that it was a 
genuine revival.  I believe that there were genuine signs and genuine wonders.  But friends, our examination is based upon this one thing: ―Was that the return of 
Pentecost, or not?‖ That is the big question here. With all the sincerity, all the great men, all the signs, all the wonders and everything that happened in 1906, our 
[px]examination involves, ―Was that truly a return of Pentecost? Was that the Seal of God? Was that the Fullness of the Spirit? Was that the Kingdom of God? Was 
that the Capstone coming to the Church?‖ This is our argument from here until.   
I  can scripturally  prove that  to  the  World (and  I  say  this  humbly)  that  when  these  messages  go  out,  I  challenge  the  world  upon  the  things  that  I  am 
saying here this morning and have spoken in the past and will be speaking in the future. I ask one man to come and prove to me in the Scripture that 1906 was a 
return of Pentecost. It is a total impossibility. If you cannot prove that was a return of Pentecost then you need another outpouring of the Holy Spirit like it was 
on the  day of Pentecost or you are  not going in the rapture. Now this becomes very  hard on the  ear because  people feel the  Spirit.  They  work signs and they 
work  wonders and they  have  a  personal  experience  with the  Lord. We  are  not denying that. I am talking about the article  of Pentecost.  I am talking about the 
Fullness of God. I am talking about what we are expecting today and what is needed for you and me to be translated here as children of God to go in the rapture. 
Without  this  outpouring  of  the  Holy  Spirit  there  would  be no  rapture.  Nobody  goes  in  the  rapture  without  this  outpouring  of  the  Spirit.  Now  that  is  very 
important.   
Then I would put the prophet‘s teaching upon this. Did he, the prophet of God, recognize that outpouring in 1906 as a return of Pentecost? I am here to 
tell you that he never did recognize that as the return of Pentecost. You will find that in the Church Ages, pages 377 to 380, just the way that I would place that 
this morning.   
[px]Quote W.M.B.: 377-2 This age started just after the turn of the twentieth century. As it was to be the age in which the true church would return to being 
the bride she was at Pentecost, we know that there must of necessity be a return of dynamic power. The believers sensed this in their spirits and began crying to 
God  for  a  new  outpouring  such  as  was  in  the  first  century.  What  seemed  to  be  the  answer  came  as  many  began  to speak  in  tongues  and  manifest  gifts  of  the 
Spirit. It was then believed that this was indeed the long awaited RESTORATION. It was not, for the latter rain can come only after the former rain which is the 
spring or TEACHING rain. The latter rain, then, is the HARVEST rain. How could this be the real thing when the Teaching Rain had not come? The Prophet-
Messenger who was to be sent to TEACH the people and turn the hearts of the children back to the Pentecostal fathers had not yet come. (Resume of the Ages – 
Church Age Chapter 10). 
[px]You cannot judge something until you know exactly what happened and then take what happened and bring it back through the Scriptures and examine 
the thing by the Scripture. Now anybody could get up and say, ―G.C.C. is false. Mr. Bruce is a false prophet. The people that are gathered here are a cult.‖ But 
brethren, where is the proof of it? Whatever you say, you must be able to take and put it back in the Scriptures. If I claim that you are the people of God, I must 
be able to take it and put it back in the Scripture. I testify this morning and I have testified for the past years, that you are the people of God because I can find 
your life registered in the Word of God with proper dressing, no adultery and no fornication. Just what the Bible recommends for you and me, we try with our 
whole hearts to practice. Now it is strange to your town here; it is strange to this whole country; it is strange to the world but it is Bible standard. Whatever we 
say, we must be able to put back in the Bible.   
[px]History - fathers of modern Pentecostalism 
[px]I will give you a brief history. I do not believe in preaching in history but even if I preach the history, I like to bring you on the scene. Now before 1906, 
there were people that desired this great Restoration and return of Pentecost. God bless their hearts.   
Now for this 1906 revival (I am calling it ―an outpouring of the Spirit,‖ genuine Spirit of the Lord) there were a few men that are now considered to be 
the fathers of Pentecostalism. The first one‘s name here is Parham—a  white  man.  And  then  there  was  a  second  man,  Seymour,  an  Afro-American. And  then 
there was another man called Frank Bartleman, all Trinitarians. This would mean something as we go along—all Trinitarians.   
[px]C. Parham is considered to be the father of Pentecost. They elevated him to such a place as the original founder and the father of Pentecost. Now, he 
was  a  former  Methodist  minister  in  Kansas,  USA.  He  was  a  theologian  who  opened  a  Bible  school  and  sought  a  deeper  walk  with  God.  He  formed  a  certain 
movement called ―The Holiness Movement.‖ That is very complimentary of this man. He  was Methodist (and Wesley brought a  holiness  message) but he  felt 
that the church was not walking in enough holiness, so he formed another movement and called it The Holiness Movement.   
Now, in 1900, he opened Bethel Bible College and sought to understand, ―What is the Baptism of the Holy Ghost?‖ He asked the students to find in the 
Bible the evidence of the Baptism of the Holy Ghost—the students! They concluded that ―tongues‖ are the evidence, and the initial evidence of the Holy Ghost 
Baptism.  Now  that  is  where  your  initial  evidence  of  the  Holy  Ghost  being  tongues  was  established:  before  1906,  about  1900  and  it  was  established  by  the 
students of a Bible school of the father of Pentecostalism called Parham, C. Parham. Now we find some rules. They concluded that as long as a person speaks in 
tongues, they have what they had on the day of Pentecost.   
Hunger set in and the people were hungry to return to Pentecost. They had great interest in the gifts of the Holy Ghost. They had great interest in healing 
of the body. They had great expectation of the Second Coming of the Lord.  This is recorded history. Not one word is going to be added.   
In 1901, a student of Bethel Bible College received the Holy Ghost and spoke in tongues. That was the initial evidence of the Holy Ghost, they claimed, 
that proved that she had the Holy Ghost. It was a woman. Then, after that, Parham and others received the Holy Ghost.   
In 1903, the first revival broke in Kansas, USA. After that revival in 1903, in Kansas, Parham opened a third Bible school. That was a Pentecostal Bible 
school.   
William J. Seymour: Now, another father of Pentecost comes on the scene. His name is William J. Seymour, an Afro-American. He got a hunger for 
God and he  attended the  Bible  school of Parham.  Since Parham  was a  racist,  he  put  Seymour on the  outside  to learn  what  he  was teaching!  Seymour, being a 
colored  man,  humbly  sat  outside.  (Racism  was  very  high  in  that  time  throughout  the  whole  of  America.)  And  all  the  lessons  that  Seymour  got,  he  was  sitting 
outside of the building! He was originally a Baptist, who later joined the holiness movement. He accepted five major doctrines of Parham, which were taught in 
the Bible school. Therefore, Parham planted his seed in Brother Seymour and that seed that was planted was based upon five major doctrines. These are the five 
doctrines: in order to be saved and be prepared for the rapture, you must be justified, sanctified (that means holy living), and get the Baptism of the Holy Ghost, 
(which is correct)! The fourth: the initial evidence of the Holy Ghost is to speak in tongues. That seed was planted in Seymour. Number five: the Second Coming 
of Christ is before the millennium (which is correct).   
Up to that time, Seymour did not get the Holy Ghost at Parham‘s Bible school although he believed all that Parham said. Seymour was invited to preach 
in Los Angeles by a woman preacher. By that time there were several women preachers with churches of their own and he was invited to preach in Los Angeles 
by one of them. He went on turned to the same Scripture that I read and preached on Acts 2:4 and laid out his message and confirmed to them that the latter rain 
is  being  outpoured  now  and  Pentecost  is  returning.  He  drew  that  conclusion  from  what  he  understood  from  Parham  and what  they  experienced  there.  Yet  he 
himself had nothing like an anointing or the Outpouring of the Spirit.  He would later get that.   
When he preached that, the woman preacher got annoyed because she was an unbeliever in this thing, and he was locked out from the church and he was 
locked out from the home. He was out on the streets. Then there was a wonderful brother called, Edward Lee who took him into his home. Then he left that home 
and later he went to another family, called the Asbury family. So now he abode with the Asbury family. God bless Brother Lee and God bless the Asbury‘s. Now 
we come down to 1906. In 1906, Seymour conducted prayer meetings at the Asbury‘s home, Los Angeles. It was continued prayer meetings. They were like us, 
seeking God, praying, fasting, believing in a return of Pentecost, looking for revival, realizing that the churches have many claims, they have a profession but not 
a possession. Something happened on April 9, 1906. Edward Lee, the man that took in Seymour and rescued him, received the Holy Ghost first.  
[px]1906: 312 Azusa street revival AND DIVISIONS 
[px]You see our attitude and our respect to God‘s servants, how it actually worked out for this man? Edward Lee received the Holy Ghost first of all other 
men because he showed respect to that little servant of God, that black man that maybe he was not even supposed to take in his home according to the laws but 
he  placed him in  his home. That  happened on  April 9, 1906 as the  first brother. In the  evening  service,  six others received it,  which included a  lady  who  was 
going to be the wife of Seymour a little later. That broke a revival in the Asbury‘s home. Then on April 12, Seymour received the Holy Ghost. After all those 
people  got  it,  down  to  his  future  wife,  he  now  received  the  Holy  Ghost.  It  was  a  genuine  article,  I  am  saying.  This  was  the  blessings  of  the  Lord.  This  was  a 
genuine revival. This was a great blessing that God sent. Only one thing was involved and this was the permissive will of God working there, which later I could 
explain. 
When that revival broke and they started speaking in tongues there were so many people gathered that that house could not contain them. So Seymour and his 
group rented an African Methodist Episcopalian church. (We have them in Trinidad up to now.) That building that they rented was at 312 Azusa Street, Los 
Angeles. That is where that great Azusa Street story came from. That was the official mission of Seymour. They rented that old abandoned building for four 
dollars a  month and they prepared it, cleaned it up and started meetings in that  place. Now,  from there  the  Pentecostal revival  was identified in 312 Azusa 
Street, and Seymour was looked upon as a revivalist and the father of Pentecostalism.  
So it was identified with 312 Azusa Street. It became the most popular address and then it gained international fame. So 312 Azusa Street is called the 
―cradle of Pentecostalism.‖ It went on for a long time. Let me tell you something, Friend. People from all parts of United States, Canada and later from around 
the world came to see that Outpouring of the Holy Spirit and revival that broke in 1906. What will it be when this great Outpouring of the Holy Spirit comes and 
the true return of Pentecost does come to the Church? It does not matter  what kind of building that you have. It does not matter what‘s your color. It does not 
matter what your education is. It does not matter what your race is, friend. When God sets a hunger in the hearts of the people and He does this thing, they will 
come from every quarter of the world.  We are looking for this thing and it certainly is going to come to pass. Do you believe these things? [Congregation says, 
―Amen!‖] Well, you better believe it because I am quoting history up to now. This is history. 
All right. William Seymour was the pastor of 312 Azusa Street. He never claimed to be a prophet or anything like that. He was the central figure of the 
1906 revival.  Now,  Luther  was  the  central  figure  of  that  revival  in  his  time.  Wesley  was  the  central  figure  of  that  revival. You see,  brethren,  it  comes  from  a 
certain source.  
He was God‘s chosen vessel for the 1906 worldwide revival that is called the Pentecostal revival. It attracted people from worldwide. Blacks and whites 
were united on  account  of  that  revival  and  that  Outpouring  of  the  Spirit.    So  these  men  actually  believed  that  Pentecost  had  returned.  They  hardly  had  any 
money; they beat a little tambourine, played a little guitar.   
Today we have organs; we have pianos; we have trumpets. Oh, we have a nice covering here and we have nice seats, but those were genuine Pentecostal 
people.  Another  thing  that  I  may  mention  here,  they  dressed  just  like  you  sisters  and  even  up  to  1950  we  have  pictures  and  we have  films  of  the  dressing  of 
Pentecostal  women  and  they  dressed  just  like you.  I  was  in  that  history  up  to  that  time  in  the  1960s,  right  in  the  Caribbean,  in  the  Pentecostal  church.  They 
dressed with the same dresses like you have and anybody who committed adultery, fornication or misbehaved, they threw them out of the church.  
Now here in the congregation is a former Pentecostal lady. This lady here, Sister Boxhill, from a child, was identified with Pentecost and she knows that 
what I am saying is the truth. I am a witness of that and there are others here who had belonged to that movement back there and they could bear witness of the 
same. They lived a moral and holy life. That was the way they were taught to dress. If that was right at that time, it is right today. Yes sir. What has changed it?  
Modernism! Hollywood has changed these things but the Church does not change with Hollywood and change with modernism. No, my brother and sisters, the 
Church of God goes on with the Word of God. We are established upon the Word of God.  
Now this may shock you a little bit here. Seymour, having respect for Parham, who was located in Texas, sent a message down to Parham and told him 
to ―Come and run a few days meetings as you may have more understanding, more knowledge. You are my teacher; I learned under your ministry. We have a 
great revival that broke over here at Azusa Street. Come and preach for us.‖ So Parham went down there. But when he went down there, he abused the people. 
He denounced the revival as a "darky camp meeting‖ and said, ―"God is sick at His stomach!" and "what good can come from a self-appointed Negro prophet?" 
And he fumed against the very African-American style of loose enthusiasm which was the norm for even Azusa's whites (Their method of worship). 
[px]The people were speaking in tongues and manifesting the gifts of the Spirit and worshipping God in the way that is called heresy and he cracked down 
on that and rebuked their style of worship then he demanded to have the full authority over the congregation. 
From all recorded history, Seymour was a humble servant of God. He was not found in adultery or fornication. Did not seek popularity nor money. He 
believed  in  Holiness.  He  was  so  humble  that  he  did  not  criticize  men  that  were  in  the  wrong  or  those  who  preached  contrary  to Bible  doctrine.  He  was  not 
militant  in  this  respect  like  the  apostles  or  Jesus  in  their  ministration.  This  caused  false  doctrine  and  evil  spirits  to  flourish.  It  eventually  organized  the  Azusa 
street mission under the title ―Apostolic faith mission‖.  
The  people  at  Azusa  Street  would  make  no  adjustment  for  that  kind  of  spirit,  and  do  you  know  what  happened?  Seymour  locked  him  out.  Seymour 
bolted the door. The next day when he came back to preach, Seymour had locked the door! ―Out!‖ (I guess he learned that from when he was locked out by this 
woman preacher.)  After he locked him out,  this  man, desiring to be  number one in the  Pentecostal  movement and revival,  went a little  way off and opened a 
church. God never blessed it. It failed before it got started and he had to shut down and walk in shame. 
Bear  in  mind,  brethren,  I  am  talking  about  the  fathers  of  Pentecost,  1906.    Here  is  one  locking  out  the  next  one!    Is  it  real one  accord?  Is  that  real 
oneness?  My, I tell you! I can‘t get it! I don‘t get the idea.  
So  Seymour  locked  out  Parham,  the father of  Pentecost,  and  they  were  separated,  never  to  be  rejoined.  Soon  after  that  revival,  in  1907  Parham  was 
charged for committing sodomy with young males. He was an angry racist and he became a full-fledged racist and was a Klan supporter. Now this man claimed 
to have received what they had on the day of Pentecost. I cannot read that in the book of Acts, where Peter, James and John or any one of them were Sodomites. 
So definitely Parham did not get what Peter, James and John had. I don‘t doubt that he got something. It is evident that his nature was not changed.  
Frank Bartleman: There was another figure that was playing out a very important part there and his name  was Frank Bartleman. When he came and 
saw that revival, first of all he denounced it but then he started to propagate that revival throughout the world as a journalist. So the news of the revival at 312 
Azusa Street went around the world and brought people in because he was a popular reporter and one that the people had confidence in since 1904. So he played 
a great part but a little later on in his writing of history he  tried to inject himself into the 1906 revival as one of the  main figures who brought that revival, it‘s 
pride!  
A book was written entitled ―what happened on Azusa street‖ based upon records of Bartleman‘s diary. He highly complimented Seymour as the main 
figure  of  the  Azusa  street  revival.  However,  he  injected  himself  in  a  subtle  manner  into  the  1906  revival  as  the  main  figure who prepared the  way  for  it.  He 
credited himself by indicating that by his preparation, the 1906 revival broke and also after the revival fires slowed down he continued that revival. Much pride 
was  manifested  in  his  records.  He  claimed  to  have  received  the  Holy  Spirit  and  spoke  in  tongues  only  several  years  after  1906.  He supported  Durham  and 
commended him as a great revivalist, though he broke Seymour‘s group.  
The gifts were restored to the Church. The Holy Ghost indeed was poured out. Healings, signs and wonders took place. People spoke in tongues. They 
accepted it as the initial evidence of the Holy Spirit. It was a genuine move of God. But then, our question is, ―Will it compare with original Pentecost?‖  Now 
this is Pentecostalism versus Pentecost. Can we fit that in the Bible? Right here, this congregation of people being taught.... You understand the Bible; you sat 
under  correct  teaching  for  the  past  forty  years.  Correct  teaching  was  going  on  here  before  some  of  you  young  fellows  were  born.  It  never  changed  and  it  will 
never  change.  We  have  been  looking  for  this  Promise of  God  for  decades  upon  decades.  Young  men  became  old  men.  Little  boys  became  grandfathers,  but 
though the vision linger, it shall surely come to pass. We buried several pastors and God raised up new ones. But, though the vision linger, it shall surely come to 
pass. God has His appointed time in which He  will send that Promise and we are closer to that Promise today than when we first begun. Yes, let us appreciate 
what we have. We have one hundred percent Truth. One hundred percent Word. 
Now this was being noised abroad in Canada and United States. It brought people from all parts of the world to see that revival and that Outpouring of 
the Holy Spirit. Now that gives you a little picture of what is coming. It united blacks and whites. It united people of all societies. It brought them together but 
there is an outstanding thing that took place in this revival. There were many joiners from many nations and walks of life and something happened. There were 
great religionists that came and joined themselves to this revival and there were great philosophers that came in and magicians, like Simon the Sorcerer. They 
always  want to join. Read your Bible. You will see how Simon the  Sorcerer, impressed by the  Power of God, wanted to join. These  magicians came  and then 
[px]there were lots of self appointed preachers that arose, lots of false prophets, many soothsayers and prophesiers and speakers in tongues and that brought in much 
confusion into the movement of God and divisions followed.   
Organizations followed this great movement and the big thing with this is that the father of Pentecost could not handle these things that came into the 
movement. He could not handle the sorcerer. (I hope your minds are going back to the book of Acts.) He could not handle the religionists. He could not handle 
the philosophers.  He could not handle the soothsayers and they became a great opposition to Seymour. They did a lot of wickedness and he did not know how to

evil force actually came and flooded the revival and neither Seymour nor the other men that arose had any power or discernment of spirits to handle these men. 
So they prevailed. Now this was going to later form the foundation of Pentecostalism.  
Clara Lum, the mission‘s secretary responsible for publication, was offended by Seymour‘s marriage. Her group believed that marriage was a disgrace, 
since Jesus was supposed to return soon. Seymour was denounced for his marriage. 50,000 names and addresses were stolen by her, which ended the mission‘s 
publication. She refused to return the names and addresses, even by Seymour‘s pleadings. Some suspected that she desired to marry Seymour.

manifested. So the foundation of this whole system eventually is corrupt from the beginning. Remember I am not speaking of the original people that loved the

[px]Examination: 1906 Pentecost with original Pentecostal plan and program 
[px]I am examining the  1906 Outpouring  with the  original Outpouring on the  Day of Pentecost.  First of all,  there  was a plan and program to bring this 
Outpouring on the Day of Pentecost and I will read it directly from the Bible to you. 
Isaiah 28: 8-13 <For all tables are full of vomit and filthiness, so that there is no place clean. 
Whom shall he teach knowledge? and whom shall he make to understand doctrine? them that are weaned from the milk, and drawn from the breasts.> 
(In other words, people that will humble themselves.) 
<For precept must be upon precept, precept upon precept; line upon line, line upon line; here a little, and there a little: For with stammering lips and 
another tongue will he speak to this people. 
[px]To  whom  he  said,  This  is  the  rest  wherewith  ye  may  cause  the  weary  to  rest;  and  this  is the  refreshing:  yet  they  would  not hear.  But  the  word  of  the 
LORD  was  unto  them precept  upon  precept,  precept  upon precept; line  upon  line,  line upon  line;  here  a  little,  and  there  a  little;  that  they  might go, and  fall

This is the plan and program of God that was applied before the day of  
Pentecost, before they had an outpouring of the Holy Spirit, which is the Rest. ―Wherewith  ye may cause the weary to rest.‖ 
What was God‘s plan and program? First of all, the Word came to the  
people before they had an outpouring of the Holy Spirit. That was God‘s plan and program. The Word first had to come and give them understanding, give them 
wisdom, give them light; show them how to prepare for this outpouring of the Holy Spirit. 
And how did the Lord do that? He did that by bringing the Word of God to the people, preparing their hearts, causing them to be justified, causing them 
to be sanctified and changing their natures. After that process, which lasted for several years, then they were commissioned to go to a certain place and tarry for

Is that the plan of God? Was that the program of God? [Congregation says, ―Amen!‖] Was it first a Restoration of the Word of God? And who did that 
Word come to?  
First of all, we see John the Baptist came on the scene, the Elijah spirit came on the scene. After that, the Word Himself, our Lord Jesus Christ, came on 
the scene. And after that Elijah spirit appeared upon the earth, he said that he was a way maker. He was preparing the way for the Lord, and he was teaching the

They were instructed how to prepare for that outpouring of the Spirit. After such instructions and the people‘s hearts were prepared, they went up into

the Seal of God, which is the Fullness of God. Then came that Outpouring on the day of Pentecost after they received the Word of God, ―precept upon precept, 
line upon line, here a little, there a little,‖ only then did they get that outpouring of the Holy Spirit.  
Now,  do  you  see  why  you  have  to  suffer  long  and  hard  in  many  services?  Beloved,  because  we  try  to  get  the  Word  of  God  to  you, ―precept upon 
precept, line upon line, here a little, there a little.‖ Do you see the need of E.O.D.H. now? It is going out there, ―line upon line, here a little and there a little‖ and 
that is either going to bless a man or curse him. That‘s what Isaiah said. He said those that did not want that and did not believe that coming Promise would go

Matthew 18:3-4 <And said, Verily I say unto you, Except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.

[px]1906 Pentecostal fathers differed FROM ORIGINAL Pentecostal fathers 
[px]Now, the 1906 outpouring, brethren, varied from this plan of God that brought this outpouring on the day of Pentecost. The fathers of Pentecostalism, 
1906,  differed  from  the  fathers  of  original  Pentecost.  (From  the  history,  you  can  already  see  it.  I  don‘t  even  have  to  repeat  it.) Brethren,  these  men  were 
Trinitarians while the original Pentecostal fathers believed in One God (St. John 4:24). The original Pentecostal fathers had a revelation of the Son of Man.   
These at 1906 did not have the Word of God in place. They did not have the Truth as the early Pentecostal people had. They depended upon theology 
they depended upon Bible schools. They were Trinitarians. They believed in three gods. They brought over doctrines of Trinitarian, water baptism in that revival, 
using the titles of Father, Son and Holy Ghost. Unless you are baptized according to Act 2:38, in the Name of Jesus Christ, you are not yet baptized in Christian 
baptism. If you believe in three gods, you are not believing the way the Bible wants you to believe. You have to believe that God is one God. God is a Spirit, (St. 
John 4:24), and that Spirit came and indwelt a man called, Jesus Christ. Through His death, burial and resurrection, He went up into heaven and returned on the 
day of Pentecost as the Holy Spirit. That is it! That‘s the One God we are talking about! 
So, they  were  wrong in their  understanding of Trinitarian  gods and Trinitarian baptism.  They  had  many other contrary doctrines. I am saying that  the 
fathers of 1906 Pentecostalism were unprepared for the outpouring of the Holy Spirit. This is proven in the man called Parham, who was a Trinitarian, who was a 
racist,  who was a proud man and who was charged for sodomy. Now, I am saying, beyond one shadow of a doubt, that this man‘s nature was not changed. In 
order for you to receive original Pentecost, before you get that article your nature must be changed by a born again experience by the incorruptible Seed, which is 
the  Word of God that liveth and abideth forever, (I Peter 1:23). These fathers failed to be  prepared, therefore, they could not receive  that  Article  of Pentecost 
under those conditions.   
Let  me  say  this  right  here:  you  and  I cannot receive  that  outpouring  of  the  Holy  Spirit  nor  go  in  the  rapture  if  these  things  are  in  us.  If  we  have  the 
wrong doctrine, if you belong to an organization, if you would not live a holy life and you are living in adultery, you are living in fornication, you are living in

Pentecost  nor  the  genuine  Baptism  of  the  Holy  Ghost  in  1906 on  account  of  their  lifestyle  and  on  account  of  their  doctrine.  They  could  not.  It‘s  a  total 
impossibility!

It is a total impossibility for the people of 1906, (including the fathers) to have received the article of Pentecost. It is a total impossibility for Pentecost to

I Peter 1:23 <Being born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever.>

preacher in the rapture. This is a different thing here, friend. And the apostles and disciples had to give that up, and tarry in Jerusalem until they got the article of 
Pentecost by a changed nature. You must have a changed nature. That is the most important thing. And I believe we have that kind of a people right here under 
this ministry.   
If your nature is changed it is a total impossibility for you to miss this Outpouring of the Holy Spirit. If you‘re a believer and you‘re not a skeptic of this 
Word, your nature is changed. It shows there is faith down in there. Yes. You believe that Word. You love that Word. You support that Word. You love  your 
brothers and sisters. You respect the ministry. You respect Christ. You have reverence in His house. Yes. You are not a gossiper. You are not a talebearer. You

them up and walk away.

it is that brings that  unbelief is a  demon  from Hell.  And Eve  thought that  she  could have listened to the  unbelief  from  the  serpent and argue with him and  get 
away. And he was quoting the Word. Yes. He said, ―God said you shall die, but I am saying ye shall not surely die.‖ That was unbelief in God‘s Word. She

pastor  there,  the  pastor  would  have  pointed  out  the  devil and say,  ̳That‘s the devil!‘ But she left the house and went down the road and she met another 
preacher. Oh yes. 
[px]Anybody  that  is  spewing  unbelief  against  that  message  and  that  promise,  they  are  trying  to  take  you  through  the  tribulation.  And  that could  be  an

[px]GOD’S GIFT MAKES A WAY 
[px]That went on and in 1936 the prophet of God met those people. So he went in there, sat down and listened to the Words of Life. Now when you have a 
gift,  that  gift  makes  a  way  for  itself.  He  was  hidden  in  a  little  church  somewhere  and  God  was  making  a  way  for  His  gift  to  operate.  Watch  out,  it  is  done  in

does not even look like God, but watch out, you better obey the Lord otherwise you will pay the same price Brother Branham paid.  
He sat down there. He was the youngest preacher of all of them, and friends, they just passed on the pulpit and by that man just passing on the pulpit, 
identifying himself. All the preachers were just saying, ―My name is so and so. My name is so and so.‖ Because the place was too full and the time was short. 
And then he passed on and said, ―My name is William Branham‖, and he just  
went on. Oh yes, my brethren!  
There is something about a man with a gift who is anointed. His words carry a certain amount of influence, and people recognize that and they realize 
that is different to the fellows that they heard. You cannot get away from that. I can prove that in the Bible from Genesis to Revelation. I will use one little type. 
There  was  a  little  man  passing  down  the  road  and  saw  two  fishermen.  One  was  a  big  fisherman,  along  with  a  next  fellow.  And  this  little  fellow  just 
passed by and said, ―Follow me and I will make you fishers of men.‖ They left their old father mending the nets, and those words were so divine that they just 
left everything ―and ―forsook their nets‖, the Bible said, ―and followed Him‖ (Mark 1:17-18). 
There is something about a gift of God. It is not the human being. People learn in Bible schools how to place their words, and how to lift their voices, 
and how to bring down their voices, and how to run down the aisle and all these kinds of gadgets. Now that is a put on, that is a Hollywood show. Now but the 
real gift of God is so simple and so humble and what it is: there is power that comes from that gift and that voice, my brothers and sisters, is a voice of authority. 
Now, the man cannot put on that. You might try to talk like that man.  
You go down the road and tell two fellows, ―Follow me and I will make you fishers of men‖, and see what results you will get. Go down to the Customs 
Office and tell the Customs Officer, ―Follow me.‖ See if he will fold up his books and give up his job to follow you. No, there is a certain authority, and those 
men had not seen a miracle yet. It is just that gift working with the Holy Spirit and it influences the sheep, ―My sheep hear my voice and a stranger they would 
not follow.‖ (John 10:27). 
[px]Now in this case of Brother Branham, he just passed there as the youngest preacher and said ―My name is William Branham‖ and kept on going. But do 
you know what happened? An influence went over all those ministers and the next day they went up to the pulpit and said, ―The man that will bring the message 
this morning is William Branham.‖ And he was dressed with a tee-shirt and seersucker trousers and, he crouched down and said, ―I don‘t want them to know, 
because I am not dressed right to go preach up there.‖ So then they were searching for this William Branham all about. ―Is William Branham here? Would you 
put up your hand?‖ No hands went up. And there was a man sitting right there, and he said, ―Are you him?‖ He said, ―Yes, but shh! Don‘t tell them.‖  He said, 
―Here he is!‖ He said, ―Those people don‘t care how you are dressed, go up there!‖ When he went up there and opened his mouth and spoke the Words of Life,

[px]Judas spirit – loved money 
[px]You  cannot  doubt  that  message;  you  have  no  questions  about  it;  you  have  no  questions  about  the  prophet;  you  have  no 
questions about that man that is preaching the truth to you and you have no questions on these articles that are brought out to you. You 
just believe them!  
They becomes such a part of  you that they are just the abcs; they are just the common things because you believe. But when 
you are a skeptic of the Word and you could hear that Word, one year, two years, five years, six years, seven years, ten years and you 
still have to watch and see if the preacher is a false prophet or if he is after your money or after your daughter or after this or after that, 
you are a dead hypocrite and you may be a Judas. That is hard language, but it is the truth. 
 Check the scriptures on Judas‘ life. Though he worked signs and wonders and miracles, he was a man that hated Jesus, was 
jealous over Jesus and was trying to find fault with Him. When that alabaster box of ointment came into the picture and His feet were 
being anointed, he started to grumble that the ointment could have been sold and the money given to the poor. He got so ―righteous.‖ 
(John 12:4-6) The Bible said, ―Not that he cared for the poor, but he was a thief.‖ That is why many of these Pentecostal preachers are 
thieves; they defraud people; they beg for money and solicit money illegally from the people. Some of them went to jail for it, even 
because they had Judas‘ spirit and yet they had gifts of the Spirit; they had the baptism; they had that Outpouring and they were in a 
revival. It is the same Judas spirit. Judas shared in the first revival but there was a second revival coming and he did not make his way 
up to Pentecost.

 How could you betray the confidence of Almighty God in you, the very Messiah that the world looked for, for four thousand

 People do not appreciate the labors of a man preaching the Word, trying to redeem them, trying to bring them to Christ, trying

 That is what I was preaching to that man about yesterday for one and a half hours. I said, ―What shall it profit a man to gain the
