The reason I wanted to go was that I wanted to be saved.  At the meetings we witnessed many healings:  crippled legs were made well, deaf ears were opened, 
blind eyes were made to see and ―Water-head‖ babies were healed.  (―Hydrocephalus.‖  This is a condition where water accumulates around the brain and the 
head actually swells bigger and bigger.)  Brother Branham would tell the people to wrap a string around the baby‘s head, cut off the string and note each day how 
much less string it took to go around the baby‘s head.  They were healed all under the mighty Power of the Holy Ghost in a man.  And we heard about Brother 
Branham‘s vision of a boy, killed in an accident, who was to be raised from the dead.  (This was fulfilled in Finland some two years later.)  An outstanding 
feature of theses meetings that made a lasting impression on me was the great humility exhibited by Brother Branham.  Although we attended the meetings and 
saw these wonderful things, I did not accept salvation until, perhaps, three months later. 
During the 1948 Miami meetings, Norma‘s father, Andrew Collins, who is well known to many, received salvation, threw away his cigarettes and went 
on a fast to seek the Will of the Lord.  He became a life-long supporter of Brother Branham‘s ministry.  Also, in 1948, Brother F.F. Bosworth heard and met 
Brother Branham for the first time and, for a time, was his campaign manager. 
After I accepted salvation in 1948, I began to seek sanctification, the second ―definite work of Grace,‖ as was taught by the Church of God.  After a time 
I received an experience that I accepted as sanctification and began to seek the Baptism of the Holy Ghost at around thirteen or fourteen years old.   
[px]Those seeking the Baptism of the Holy Ghost were invited to the altar to pray.  Those who were already filled were invited to pray with them.  Various 
methods were used to help ―pray you through‖ to the Holy Ghost.  On person would tell you to say, ―Praise the Lord, Praise the Lord, Praise the Lord,‖ as fast as 
you can.  Another might tell you to say whatever came into your mind, while a third might tell you to say, ―Abba, babba, babba,‖ and just make babbling sounds 
and continue to do it until you had lost control of your tongue.  Some seekers would be so earnest and try so hard that they would work up a sweat and literally 
get sick, go out and throw up and come back to pray some more!  Sincerity! 
A few people would claim to be ―saved, sanctified and filled with the Holy Ghost‖ all in one night.  However, many of those same people would soon 
go back to the sins that they had tried to escape—―like a dog to its vomit.‖  The church believed that you could be saved and lost and saved and lost any number 
of times.  Also, you could receive the Holy Ghost and lose it to go through the cycle again.  I never understood that.  It didn‘t seem to fit with Ephesians 1:13-14.  
However, I continued to seek for the Baptism.  But what I eventually received was not like the original Pentecost. 
Seemingly, all my young church friends received the Baptism after only a short time of prayer and, having received it, several announced that they had a 
―call‖ to be a missionary, an evangelist or to have some other form of ministry.  However, I did not receive the Baptism for several years (1952 or 1953), 
although I went to the altar to pray at almost every opportunity.  When, finally, although I was baptized in water in a triune baptism, I did receive the ―Baptism of 
the Holy Ghost‖—the 1906 version—but I thought it was the article of Pentecost. Following this experience, I was sure that the Spirit would be strong in me to 
fulfil John 14:12 and Mark 16:17.  But within three days, I knew something was wrong.  It didn‘t work!  It didn‘t work!  The Baptism in me did not do what It 
did in the Apostles; It did not do what Jesus said It should do and I felt no call to any ministry.  It did not do what I had seen in Brother Branham‘s ministry.  It 
didn‘t work!  There was something wrong and I didn‘t understand what it was.  But I continued to claim that Baptism of the Holy Ghost for a number of years, 
until about 1964 when I essentially made a public confession that I didn‘t have It.  That led to ―problems‖ that I have told about before.  
During my teen years, I learned that you cannot necessarily believe something just because it is written in the Sunday School lesson book or just 
because a preacher says it in a sermon.  In a Sunday School lesson concerning Jesus‘ crucifixion, the writer of the lesson text, a well-known minister of the 
church, wrote that when they nailed the hands and feet of Jesus, ―You could hear the crunch of the bones.‖  Immediately, I remembered the Scripture that says, 
―A bone of Him shall not be broken.‖  (John 19:36)  I began to learn to be critical of things that preachers said, even though I was so caught up in the church 
doctrine that I did not then question many major errors in doctrine that the church held.  It took time.  As Isaiah 28 says, ―It‘s line upon line, precept upon 
precept, here a little and there a little.  It took time—years. 
[px]MANIFESTATIONS OF “POWER” 
[px]During these years, I observed men and women shake under what was believed to be the Power of God.  Women danced until their hair pins came out 
and their hair fell down.  Sometimes men and women would be ―slain in the Spirit‖ and someone would have to come and cover the exposed legs of the women 
because they were exposed indecently.  Sometimes the men, shaking under this power would fall over the seats, the benches, the pews, whatever was there.  They 
would fall over it and things would go flying.  If people happened to be sitting there, all of a sudden they had a lap full of somebody that was ―slain under the 
Power of the Spirit.‖  Somehow those things were kind of hard to reconcile, too. 
People often spoke in ―tongues‖ but never in a language that I could understand or even recognize.  My mother‘s parents were German.  I could have 
recognized the language if I had heard it.  I would have recognized Spanish.  I think I would have recognized French.  I was in high school where French was 
[px]taught.  I never heard a language that I could understand or even recognize.  Sometimes the ―message in tongues‖ would consist of a couple of words, sometimes 
repeated.  Often such a ―message‖ would be ―interpreted‖ but the ―interpretation‖ usually was far longer than the message in tongues.  The message might go for 
two or three or half a dozen words; the interpretation might go on for five or ten minutes.  My wife can attest to this.  Occasionally, a ―message in tongues‖ 
would be lengthy, as was also the ―interpretation.  Interpretations were almost always quotations or paraphrases of Scriptures or general warnings of judgment 
coming upon the land—nothing in the way of specifics as Brother Branham taught and the way he tried to put it in place in his church. 
Sometimes the ―Spirit‖ would begin to move in the song service or in a period of testimonies.  Then praise and worship would extend until almost noon 
(on a Sunday).  Usually the minister, at that point, would get up and say that the song service or the worship was so good that there was no need for any 
preaching—―So, we‘ll let you go a little early!‖  Somehow, that seemed to put things just backwards.  As Brother Bruce was pointing out today, the Word is first.  
That is not to say that a service should not be set aside to come and worship God. 
Other times the minister would, in the course of giving his sermon, begin to speak in tongues and ―prophesy.‖  This might set off a period of unchecked 
―prophesying‖ by several people.  (―Prophesying‖ meaning speaking in unknown tongues.)  Since there was no church order to put it in place, the minister would 
have to quit his sermon and give an altar call or close the service.  People would still say how wonderful the service was even in the absence of a completed 
sermon.  You remember that just today, Brother Bruce told of women that would begin to prophesy and speak in tongues and even displace the minister from the 
pulpit! 
Often the service would degenerate into a babble of people speaking in unknown tongues with no interpretation.  As Brother Bruce pointed out, in I 
Corinthians 14, such ―babble‖ was not Scriptural.  Paul said that, if there are tongues in church, there needs to be an interpretation so that the church can be 
edified.  There might be several people speaking in tongues at the same time.  Paul said that it should be only one or two or maybe three, at the most, and that 
should be ―in order.‖  And, if there is no interpreter, no interpretation, be quiet.  Keep silence.  If there is no interpreter, let him keep silence in the church.  (I 
Corinthians 14) 
It has often been said that, among Pentecostals, their knowledge of the Scriptures is woefully inadequate.  Why?  Because they don‘t care that much 
about the Word.  They go more on feelings and emotions.  A lot of times, as Pentecostals, we would ―look down our noses‖ at Baptists and Methodists and 
Presbyterians and people like that, because ―they didn‘t believe in the Baptism of the Holy Ghost with the initial evidence of speaking in tongues.‖  But I learned, 
over the years, that those people had a better knowledge and understanding of the Scriptures, generally, than the Pentecostals had.  The Pentecostals liked their 
feelings and emotionalism better than the Word of God. 
Over time, it became obvious that the pastor‘s sermons followed some centrally organized schedule.  Often, as I learned, the sermon at the Church of 
God was essentially the same as that at the local Baptist church or the Presbyterian church and used the same Scriptures and illustrations.  That was often the 
case.  And then I found that many ministers purchased their sermon outlines—some through the church official documents and some through other organizations 
that weren‘t even Pentecostals. 
[px]MONEY COLLECTIONS 
[px]There was a strong emphasis on money in the Church of God, and, I am sure, in the other Pentecostal churches (and in most churches, generally, I 
think.)  There seemed to be a preoccupation with money, especially the getting of it.  There often seemed to be some project that required money—a building 
[px]addition, a new piano or organ, a new sound system, a new Sunday School bus, money to pay for Radio broadcasts, money to send the pastor, the youth director, 
their wives and certain youths to special youth meetings, money to send the pastor‘s family to the annual ―camp meeting‖ or to the general conference of pastors 
and overseers, or money to pay visiting evangelists or to send to the mission fields.  There was no end of ―needs‖ for money.  Seemingly, none of these things 
could be budgeted to come out of the general fund of tithes and offerings.   
To pay for special ―needs,‖ a deacon or minister would go before the congregation and tell the people, first, how good they were, how wonderful it was 
that they were so generous and willing to help with past projects.  They would then explain the present ―need‖ and how much was required.  Then they would ask 
for pledges beginning with $100.  After collecting several $100 bills, they would go to $50 and then $20 or even $10.  After this, the offering plate would be 
passed to collect all the odds and ends, if there was any left.  While the pledges were being made they would have someone counting the money as it came in 
and, if not enough was given, they would start over again—the same day!  Pledges did not usually have to be paid on the spot but people were encouraged to give 
what they did not have and often what they had no prospect of getting short of taking a second job or sending the wife out to work.  There were often calls for a 
―silent offering.‖  They did not want to bother with loose change, they wanted bills.  We think, ―Well, a dollar is not worth much.‖  It was worth a lot more in 
those days.  In the 1930s and in the early 1940s, you could buy a week‘s groceries for a family of four for five dollars.  You can‘t fill a grocery bag for five 
dollars now.  A silent offering, they didn‘t want to bother with the change.  Seemingly, they forgot that Jesus commended that widow woman that put her two 
mites in the offering.  He said that she had given more than them all.  (Mark 12:42). 
[px]DONUT SALES 
[px]During the mid-1960s, we attended a Jesus‘ Name church in Tampa.  Since the church ―needed‖ a school bus to bring children and others to Sunday 
School, it was determined to raise the money by selling donuts.  Each Saturday, several adults would take a few children and quite a few boxes of donuts in their 
car, go to several different neighbourhoods and they would send the children door to door to sell donuts.  Over a period of time, this bought the Sunday school 
bus. 
I recall that Brother Bob Lambert said something about donuts.  He said that, in Louisiana, a donut shop was completely ruined by a church going into 
the donut business and one furniture store operator was heard to say that he hoped the church did not decide to go into the furniture business!   
Pentecostalism!  Those things were almost fifty years ago.  What is it now? 
[px]BIRTHDAY OFFERINGS 
[px]Those celebrating birthdays at the Church of God were asked to put in one penny for each year of life.  Some churches had a small replica of a church 
that they would bring out and set on the altar.  There was a bell inside so placed that when the pennies were dropped, the bell would ring.  The congregation 
would count and see how old you were.  Some of the more ―mature‖ people circumvented this.  They would hold the pennies in a certain way so that when they 
[px]dropped, they would go ―r-r-r-r-r-r-r-r-ring,‖ so no one could count.  And they would sing ―Happy Birthday.‖  The pennies so collected were to be used for 
missions.  The average birthday offering on a given Sunday was probably not much more than a dollar. 
[px]DAVID TERRILL’S GUITAR CASE 
[px]Tent ―evangelist,‖ David Terrill, held a meeting in Tampa around 1969.  He preached and prophesied and played his guitar.  Brother Carlton McGahee 
wanted to see Terrill and give him a Church Age Book with the hope of influencing him to follow Brother Branham‘s message.  He and I, together with our 
families, went to an evening service and witnessed an amazing sight.  Following several congregational songs, Terrill came to the platform (which was about five 
feet high from the floor) bringing a guitar case.  He took out the guitar and began to play it as he sang portions of a song and to ―prophesy.‖  All of the prophecy 
concerned money and how that the economy was going to fail, that the money people had in their bank accounts would be no good and that they should put it 
into the ministry of the Gospel—his ministry.  He ―discerned‖ that someone out in the audience had a $100 bill in their billfold and that it should be put into the 
offering.  He ―prophesied‖ that he would put his guitar case on the platform floor and that the people would fill it up!  You know, David Terrill was a true 
prophet.  He went on for forty-five minutes or more in this vein as the people under the tent walked forward and put their cash, their checks and their ―I.O.U.s‖ 
into the case.  Before they were finished, the money could be seen mounded up higher than the side of the guitar case!  Brother McGahee and I and our families 
witnessed that. 
Terrill‘s pull for money should be contrasted with Brother Branham, who said he never took up an offering in his life.  In fact, more than once, Brother 
Branham ―fired‖ people who made a pull for money in his campaigns.  One of the people that he ―fired‖ was the wife of F.F. Bosworth, who may have been his 
campaign manager at the time.  The meeting was being held at a Christian Missionary Alliance church in Miami in 1949.  She made a big pull for money and He 
said, ―That‘s enough of that.  We won‘t do that any more.‖ 
[px]SUNDAY SCHOOL CLASS OFFERINGS 
[px]After we had been living in Miami for a few years, around 1953 we still had a lot of family in the Arcadia, Florida area and I visited the Arcadia 
Church of God once again, the same church where I had been as a small boy.  We were there for a Sunday service so I joined a Sunday School class of older 
teenagers and young adults.  At the beginning of the class, the teacher, a woman, took the roll and asked someone to take up the class offering after explaining 
the current need.  When that was done, she asked someone else to give a report on the class fund that was to be used to pay for class social activities.  They 
discussed some things that might be done and took up another offering.  When that was finished, someone remembered the ―flower fund.‖  So, another report 
was given, another discussion and another offering collected.  When this was done, the bell rang signalling the end of the class period.  They didn‘t so much as 
read a verse of Scripture of the lesson that was supposed to have been studied during that forty-five minute period!  In giving her apology for not getting to the 
Sunday School lesson, the teacher said, ―Well, these things are important, too!‖  Money!  Preoccupation with ―money.‖ 
[px]PASTOR APPRECIATION DAY AND LOVE OFFERING 
[px]Pastor Appreciation Day was observed once a year.  Some official would tell how great and wonderful the pastor and wife were and then call the people 
to show their appreciation by making an offering and coming forward to pin it to their clothes.  Naturally, everybody would want to be seen to love and support 
their pastor and his wife.  Contrast this with Matthew 6:1-4: 
<¶ Take heed that ye do not your alms before men, to be seen of them: otherwise ye have no reward of your Father which is in heaven. 
Therefore when thou doest [thine] alms, do not sound a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may 
have glory of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward. 
But when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:  
That thine alms may be in secret: and thy Father which seeth in secret himself shall reward thee openly.> 
[px]PASTOR’S SALARY AT MIAMI CHURCH OF GOD 
[px]The Church of God pastor‘s salary is based upon church membership. 
Sometime around 1950 my father ―got saved‖ again and began regular church attendance.  After a time, he was appointed to the church‘s Finance 
Committee.  At one meeting, he raised the question concerning many members being listed on the church rolls who had not attended church in years, some living 
in ―open sin‖ and some were not even known to the members of the Finance Committee.  It had been a long time since they had been to church.  My father 
thought that the membership rolls should be cleaned up and inactive names be eliminated.  This evidently pinched the preacher a little and he commented, ―Those 
people were members before I came and they‘re gonna be members when I leave!‖ 
―Pentecostalism!‖  I think you get the gist of it.  These things illustrate some of the things that Brother Bruce has been talking in the several messages 
that we have heard lately.  What he has been saying is right on line.  It‘s the truth.  Pentecostalism that developed out of that 1906 revival was based upon faulty 
doctrine, faulty standards, faulty foundations to start with.  We could talk about sexual misconduct, dishonesty among preachers, etc. 
We appreciate that the Lord has seen fit to use us in a small way to support this ministry that is going out through the E.O.D.H. books, the Expositions 
of Damnable Heresies, around the world.  We thank the Lord that we‘re elected to be a part of that.  We look for a fulfilment of God‘s Promise at any time.  
Praise God! 
[px]HOLINESS 
[px]Hebrews 12:14 <Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord.> 
Holiness is a big word and we who are trying to live a holy life have our work cut out for us.  All around us, every day, is all sorts of unholiness, all 
sorts of sins and iniquities.  The Bible says, ―As it was in the days of Noah, so shall it be at the coming of the Son of Man.  As it was in the days of Sodom and 
Gomorrah, so shall it be....‖  And we see that all around us.  The Bible says that, in the days of Noah they married, they gave in marriage, they builded, they did 
all sorts of things.  They lived unholy lives.  Not that there is anything necessarily wrong with marriage.  Paul said that marriage is honorable and the bed is 
undefiled but there must have been something dishonorable in what they were doing in the days of Noah.   
Many of the Pentecostal churches have taken it upon themselves to call themselves, ―Holiness Churches.‖  At one time in the early days of ―Holiness,‖ 
that fit.  It fit very well because they were very strict in they way they acted, the way they appeared, they way they dressed, the way they conducted their lives, 
but, I‘m afraid, in too many cases that has long since been forgotten. 
[px]LET-DOWN OF HOLINESS STANDARDS 
[px]All of you have heard of Oral Roberts and his ministry.  He had a very powerful ministry that came after Brother Branham.  In fact, he went to Brother 
Branham and asked if he thought the Lord would bless him in a ministry.  Brother Branham told him to go ahead.  Around 1951 or 1952, my mother was healed 
of a severe and very painful case of arthritis in his meeting.  You heard from Brother Bruce how his stand degenerated into a call for $8 million or God would kill 
him and how he, in later life, embraced the Methodist church.   
In the early 1950s, Oral Roberts‘ father and mother visited Miami, probably in a denominational Pentecostal Holiness church.  They were members of 
the Pentecostal Holiness organization and he was a minister.  They seemed like very sincere people, very nice, very humble; dressed modestly.  I don‘t recall the 
subject of the sermon but Brother Roberts preached a message that we appreciated. 
By the 1950s, and particularly in the urban churches, holiness standards had begun to be let down.  Women began to wear high-heeled shoes and you 
know some of the things that Brother Branham said about high-heeled shoes—it throws the shape of your body off and it causes men to lust after you women.  It 
also does things to your body that will cause leg and back problems.  People began to wear jewellery on their clothes, a pin, a brooch, a necklace.  Sometimes a 
wedding band was to be seen.  Men began to wear flashy watchbands.  Young men began to participate in organized sports at local high schools—football, 
basketball, baseball.  Somehow, in my coming through high school those things did not attract me.  I thank the Lord for it.  And it was frowned on.  It was 
frowned on but people began to do it anyway.  The people who did it were not chastised; they were not thrown out of church or anything like that.  They just 
went their own way and did it.  But they were still ―Christians.‖  Remember that the Pentecostals placed more emphasis on feelings and emotions than the Word 
of God.   
[px]In the mid 1960s, we observed young women, older girls, wearing short skirts, several inches above the knees, to church.  That was right along the line 
of something Brother Branham said.  Somebody asked him a question, one time, about ―scandal skirts.‖  He inquired to find out what a ―scandal skirt‖ was.  
Apparently this was a combination of a long ruffled underskirt (slip or petticoat) worn under a shorter outer skirt so the ruffled slip can be seen.  Brother 
Branham made a comment about that.  He said he couldn‘t understand why a woman would want to go around with her underclothes showing.  I think that is still 
good to remember. 
Recently at a large Pentecostal church that I know of, there were men, apparently officials in the church, wearing their long hair in pigtails, braided 
pigtails down their back, not real long but they were there.  Some women, on the other hand, had very short hair, cut really close.  If you didn‘t notice what kind 
of clothes they were wearing, you weren‘t sure whether they were men or women.  Women wore lipstick and other makeup.  Many women wore dresses but they 
were often very tight, very revealing.  Many women wore shorts or pants.  At least one person, a grown woman, had on what I would call a ―playsuit.‖  And 
when the ―Spirit‖ began to move in the church and people all around began to speak in tongues and ―prophesy,‖ this woman in the playsuit began to speak in 
tongues and ―prophesy.‖ Some men were observed wearing gold chains and earrings.  Women wore earrings and gaudy finger rings.  The choir members all had 
on fancy robes. 
In the late 1940s, after one of my uncles was saved, he came to the Lord, he had a television set.  Do you remember what Brother Branham said he 
would do if he had one?  My uncle literally did that.  He took it out in the yard, got his shotgun out and shot the thing!  He knew television was wrong and he 
never again had one.  Now, however, most Pentecostals have their television sets. 
[px]WOMEN PREACHERS 
[px]As long as I can remember, there were women preachers in the Church of God and the pastor‘s wife at a Jesus‘ Name church in Tampa that we attended 
in the mid-1960s was also an ordained minister.  I noticed that, while these women were ordained ministers, in most of those churches you did not often see them 
preach.  I suspect, although I never heard it discussed very much, that they realized that it was a controversial issue and that the Bible has some things to say 
about that.  Paul said, ―I suffer not a woman to teach nor to usurp authority over the man.‖  He said, ―Let a woman be in subjection.‖  How can a woman be in 
subjection and be up there preaching trying to exhort the whole congregation?  I Timothy 2:12 strictly prohibits it.   
We noticed that if the leader of the assembly has a particular problem, for example, he or she was divorced and remarried, then there is likely to be a 
large number of people in the congregation with the same problem.  Whatever spirit is on the leader attracts similar spirits among the congregation.  If you find a 
congregation where the pastor is preaching under the Spirit of God, you are going to find people that want to hear the things that God spoke and the things that 
will make you eligible to go in the rapture.  We don‘t want any of those evil spirits around here.  So, if there is a divorced woman who remarried and is pastoring 
a church, there likely will be a high percentage of divorced women in the church and, possible, not very many men.  And we have seen that. 
[px]SEXUAL MISCONDUCT AMONG THE MINISTRY 
[px]In Miami, a woman, a preacher, although I don‘t recall ever hearing her preach, was also a lesbian.  It seemed to be common knowledge that she was a 
sexual pervert, even though the church had strict rules against that.  But no one would investigate the matter.  No one, no officials would do anything about it.  
They left her strictly alone—and she preyed on some of the younger sisters in the church, especially those who didn‘t have any parents in the church.  She would 
make friends with those. 
There was a pastor of a large urban Pentecostal church in Tampa Florida where my sister went and still goes. Three or four years ago he gave a heretical 
sermon.  I was there and when he gave the title of his sermon, I realized that he was ―barking up the wrong tree.‖  He was dead wrong.  When he took his text, I 
just flipped over in my Bible.  He told the people they didn‘t have to follow him in their Bibles, but I looked at the chapter and glanced down the verses and I saw 
a verse that totally destroyed his entire sermon—in the very chapter that he was using.  One verse destroyed the whole sermon.  But he went ahead, he preached 
verse by verse—from memory.  Every time he finished with a verse he would go on to the next one, repeat the verse from memory and comment on it to support 
his thesis, his primary point—until he came to that one verse.  I wondered what he would do with it.  Very simple.  He skipped it!  He could do that because 
nobody was looking on, nobody was reading their Bibles. You don‘t have to worry about that here.  We‘re not going to bring a message and find out down the 
page and our whole message is destroyed.  No.  No.  You don‘t have to worry about that here.  When he skipped that verse and went right on the next on, He 
never skipped a beat, just went right on.  I said to myself, ―There is a dishonest preacher!‖  In less than two years he saw fit to run off with a man‘s wife.  He left 
his own wife, ran off with the other man‘s wife.  This is the pastor of a big church, Pentecostals, ―Holy Ghost‖ filled preacher!  He could quote Scripture, as 
Brother Branham said, ―Like peas rolling down a dry cowhide.‖  It nearly destroyed the congregation.   
That‘s not the end of the story.  The church officials got together.  This was a big problem.  What were they going to do about this man?  This man is 
very smart, very bright, very influential, had a great program going at the church and was very well looked up to in the neighbourhood, in the community.  The 
church officials put together a program to rehabilitate this preacher, this minister.  They were going to rehabilitate him within two years so that he could go back 
to the ministry—but he refused.  He didn‘t want to wait two years.  He left the church; he went down the road and started a new church.  I don‘t know how they 
ever put that in the Scripture.  I don‘t know how they excused themselves for trying to bring back somebody like that.  It‘s wrong.  But, apparently, that is the 
way they do things. 
[px]PASTOR APPOINTMENT 
[px]In the Church of God, pastor appointment, ―appointment‖ is for two years.  At the end of two years the pastor either leaves and goes somewhere else, or 
the church might vote on him and reappoint him for another two years.  If the church is tired of the way he preaches and his sermons are too long or they pinch 
too hard, they might just have the deacon board tell him that he won‘t be reappointed.  So he leaves and they begin to look around for somebody that is more to 
their liking.  The get names; they call the state general overseer and they get names of preachers that are available and they begin to interview these preachers, 
get their resumes, and settle on two or three.  They will invite them for a weekend of services.  The preacher comes and preaches Sunday morning and Sunday 
evening services and goes back home.  After the deacon board has heard two or three of these candidates over successive weekends, they get together and decide 
to ―invite‖ one of the preachers to be the pastor.  Or, if they are not satisfied, they might ask for more candidates.  (That‘s not the way Paul told Titus to do it.)  
When they call him and offer him the position of pastor of thus-and-thus church and he ―prays‖ about it.  He waits a few days.  He‘s got to fast and pray about 
this—and see if he gets another letter in the mail from some other church with a better offer.  That‘s the way it‘s done. 
[px]MINISTERS JOKING 
[px]Probably the most filthy joke that I ever heard in my entire life—I was in the U.S. Army for three years and I heard people tell things—but, probably, 
the most filthy joke that I ever heard in my entire life was from the pastor of a Pentecostal church.  I can tell you that whatever respect I had for that man was 
destroyed.   It was destroyed.  That was quite a few years ago. 
One time there was a Pentecostal group that had an older building but it was in serviceable condition, I thought.  The pastor got a letter from the city 
zoning board.  The letter stated that the building had been inspected, was in need of repairs, the cost of repair was more than the value of the building, therefore, 
the building had to be torn down and removed and the lot cleared within thirty days.  I saw the letter.  I read the letter to the congregation.  I had it in my hands.  I 
wish I had made a copy of it.  The pastor, the next day went down to the city officials to inquire about this and find out, ―Is this really true?‖  They looked at it 
and said, ―Yes sir, it‘s true.  Your building is going to cost more to repair than it‘s worth so it must be torn down in thirty days.‖  ―Well, who can I talk to.‖  
―Well that‘s the way it is.  You are going to have to do it.‖  Then the pastor got a little hot under the collar and he said, ―Well, I‘ll have you know that I am a 
member of an organization that has churches in forty different countries and we have papers and magazines in every one of them and I‘ll see that a copy of this 
letter goes in every one of them!‖  So they said, ―Oh, well, Reverend, let me see that.‖  They took the letter back in the other room and in a few minutes they 
came back and said, ―Reverend, it was all a mistake.  It was all a mistake.  That letter should never have gone out!‖  The church was not torn down—at least, not 
then.   
Later, I questioned the pastor about that and he gave me an answer that I thought was very unusual.  What I was questioning was his calling on the 
power of his organization.  He claimed to be an independent Pentecostal preacher, a Jesus‘ Name preacher.  When confronting the zoning board, he called on this 
organization, the IMA.  He said, ―Well, I found out I had to have a covering for my head.‖  ―I found out I had to have a covering for my head!‖  Now, what does 
the Bible say about a covering for the head?  Our covering for  
our head is Christ—not some man-made organization. 
Norma and I used to loan out Spoken Word books to some of the people in the congregation if they showed any interest at all.  Some sounded like they 
might be interested and we‘d loan the books.  When the books didn‘t come back and we would ask about them.  ―Well, we never got around to reading it.‖  
―Well, I don‘t know just where it is.  Maybe I can find it.‖  We told people that we had tapes of Brother Branham.   We gladly would have loaned those around to 
people to hear them.  Nobody had any interest in those tapes or in the books.  After a while we decided that we were wasting our time.  So we didn‘t bother any 
more. 
[px]AMERICANA HOTEL CONVENTION, 1961 
[px]Those of you who have read the ―First Martyr‖ Book have seen some of this but I‘ll say it for the benefit of those who may listen to this tape or 
whoever might read this testimony in the new E.O.D.H. book that is being prepared now.   
[px]The Full Gospel Business Men‘s Fellowship International (FGBMFI) had a convention in the Americana Hotel in Miami Beach, Florida, about May of 
1961.  This was right after Brother Bob Lambert had been in Sarasota, Florida, at Gerald Derstine‘s convention.  Brother Bob had gone down there under the 
leading of the Lord.  The Lord met him there and many of you have seen a copy of the tract that was written describing his experience, ―God Spoke.‖  That was 
at the end of January 1961.   
Norma‘s parents attended.  We happened to be in Miami, at the time, and we determined to attend the FGBMFI convention.  Brother Bob Lambert was 
there.  He had been invited to preach but they did not let him preach.  I guess somebody heard that he had some controversial doctrine about Baptism in Jesus‘ 
Name and people didn‘t have the Holy Ghost the way they claimed.  In their meetings, they had a number of ―converts.‖  On Sunday afternoon, they planned to 
baptize these people.  On Miami Beach, the nearest fresh water (more or less) was in the hotel swimming pool.  The hotel did not allow anyone who was clothed 
to go into the swimming pool; they had to wear a bathing suit.   
The announcement was made that there would be a baptism service in the swimming pool but everyone would have to wear a bathing suit!  Norma and I 
heard about it, probably being invited, but we determined not to go.  We did not go.  That was an evidence of a corrupted excuse for a holy sacrament of God—
one of the sacraments—baptism in water.  We are commanded to baptize but we are not commanded to go out and make ourselves naked before the world!   
Brother Bob said, ―I was so disappointed at the Americana convention.‖  He said, ―Shame, shame, shame upon those ministers down there, public 
bathing, it‘s a disgrace to our God.  Carried their Jesus down there in the presence of those ungodly women, naked, bathed themselves and talked and chatted 
with them.  Even invited me.  I shuddered and was scared to my shoes.  My toes would curl up.‖ 
And he was talking to some young minister and he said, ―Brother, you don‘t have enough money to hire me to come in public swimming with you.  Son, 
you‘re not fit to preach the Gospel of the Lord Jesus Christ, if you don‘t know that much.  If your Holy Spirit in you hasn‘t possessed you enough that you‘re not 
sensitive enough in the Spirit to something that terrible, Brother, you had better get back to your prayer closet and make you a contact with God.‖  Full Gospel 
Business Men! 
Brother Branham preached a message, ―The Seed Is Not Heir With The Shuck,‖ and he talked about how the corn comes up with the sprout, the stalk 
and the tassel, etc.  I think it was Brother Branham that someone asked, ―You know, you talked about Luther and Wesley and Pentecostals, what is the Full 
Gospel Businessmen?‖  Brother Branham is said to have replied, ―Well, that‘s the fungus on the shuck!‖   
I think that pretty well covers my testimony.  There are many other things that could be said.  We are looking for something real.  We are looking for a 
return of the same Pentecostal experience that the one hundred and twenty had on the Day of Pentecost, almost 2000 years ago.  The substitute that the 1906 
―Pentecostal Fathers‖ accepted will not take us in the rapture; indeed it will not even keep many from smoking, lying, cheating, stealing or committing adultery 
and fornication.  The Prophet, Joel, spoke of an Outpouring of the Spirit of God to come in the last days.  The Apostle, Peter, who spoke to the crowd on the Day 
of Pentecost, said that ―This Promise is unto you and to your children and to them that are afar off even as many as the Lord our God shall call.‖  That means 
even down to our day.  To accept anything less than what they received is to condemn our own souls to the great tribulation. Amen! 
[px]ALDWYN CHANDLER – MINISTER &  
SECURITY OFFICER 
[px]I am a citizen of Trinidad & Tobago by birth. I lived a life of drugs and sexual immoralities. At the 
age of seventeen I was at home when I had an experience with the Lord that led me to repentance. While on 
my knees weeping, my sins came up before me. I wept until my heart became soft. My desires for drugs and 
immoral living left. I just wanted to serve the Lord. A few days after, a cousin invited me to a crusade at 
the Church of God Deliverance Centre. I accepted the Lord Jesus as my Savoir and it was a wonderful 
feeling; my sins were forgiven. I felt at peace with God.  
I never saw people like that. I only knew about Baptist, Seventh Day Adventist, Anglican and 
Catholic. I witnessed singing and dancing in the Spirit, speaking in tongues and interpretations. There 
were prophecies, greeting one another with a holy kiss; brothers and sisters, both young and old. To me, 
that was love. I was baptized in the titles of Father, Son and Holy Ghost. They preached about the Holy 
Ghost and I wanted it more than ever, for I was powerless. 
There was a sister from Tobago who came to us for deliverance from demons. They were grunting as pigs 
and I, with other ministers, was in prayer and fasting. They had the Holy Ghost but I did not. They left me 
with this sister. She was big and fat and in her fifties. All I had to do was command the devils to work 
and they would start to make the woman pass out something like mucus, which was green, yellow and off-white 
in colour. I emptied buckets of it. The demons spoke with a man’s voice and one said to me, “You have a 
commanding  spirit,  but  you  better  get  the  Holy  Ghost.  Why  don’t  you  go  and  cast  the  devil  out  of  your 
brother who is in prison?” How they knew those things, I did not know, but it was the truth, for I had a 
brother who at that time was in prison. I started to plead the Blood and commanded the demons to work. They 
never left and she returned home. 
[px]RECEIVED 1906 BAPTISM – MANIFESTED GIFTS 
[px]Some months after, I was in a “tarry meeting” for the Holy Spirit; which I believed I received after 
speaking an unknown language. This was a great thing, for I received power to do the works of Christ. I 
started preaching on the streets and anywhere I could; going to the hospital, praying for the sick, casting 
out devils, signs, wonders and all the gifts of the Spirit working in me.  
One time, I went into a Catholic Church, which was filled to capacity. The priest was coming to the 
altar and I was walking up the aisle, calling the people to come out of Babylon. The priest fled as I stood 
there. Then two men held me by my arms, and being thrown, I was in mid air moving very fast and landed 
outside of the church. I was wrong and it was zeal without knowledge.  
I had an anointing that came on me very strongly whenever the Lord was going to use me, and It told 
me things before they came to pass, and they did. One time I was praying in the church, preparing for a 
youth rally and we had a speaker for that night, a pastor, and a wonderful preacher. The anointing came on 
me and revealed to me that I was going to preach that night. No one told me anything up until I had taken 
the offering and was in the room looking for the minister. Then the youth leader told me that I would be 
preaching, the other minister could not make it. Another time in Tobago, a brother and myself were praying, 
[px]fasting and seeking the Lord. On that Sunday morning, we were all ready and waiting for the vehicle to pick 
us up at 8:00 a.m. When it was 8.30 a.m. no car showed up. So, I told them to wait, and I went to a room 
where I prayed and asked the Lord to reveal to me, by the anointing, whether the vehicle would come for us 
or not? The anointing came and said that the vehicle would come and I would be the preacher that morning. I 
told them that the car is coming. It came and we arrived just before the time of preaching of the Word. I 
had never seen the people before and the sister told the pastor that I was from Trinidad. He went to the 
pulpit and called me to preach.  
One night, after a prayer meeting, while going home, I came out of the bus and the anointing came on 
me. I knew that the Lord was going to use me. I kept on walking and He said, “Stand up here and preach.” 
There was no one there at the corner of Charlotte and Duke Streets. I started to preach and then one young 
man came and stood up. He was a backslider. I preached with boldness and he accepted the Lord. After some 
days of praying, fasting and tarrying for the Holy Ghost, I laid hands on him and he received the baptism 
of the Holy Ghost. Today he is still serving the Lord, on that experience. That anointing would not stay 
around if I did anything unholy, but if I repented and drew near, It would come. I was “teacher, pastor, 
evangelist, prophet and apostle.” Something was wrong but I could not put my hands on it.  
One night, my sister came to church and decided to pretend that she was demon possessed, screaming 
with  a  loud  voice.  I  was  not  aware  that  she  was  pretending.  The  bishop  began  to  speak in  an  unknown 
language: “Umbadah humbadah humbadahhi”, and commanded the devil to come out. Then I leaned over her, she 
was on the ground, and I put my hands around her neck and started to command that devil to come out. I was 
choking her and did not realize until she opened one eye and said, “Aldwyn, you are choking me.” It was 
then I realized she was just acting. No one else knew what happened. I told her to say, “Jesus” and she 
really said, “Jesus”.  
[px]1906 BAPTISM DID NOT OPERATE AS THAT OF ACTS 2 
[px]There was a man who accepted the Lord after an open-air meeting. He was a bodybuilder. As we prayed 
for him, he began to vibrate and a demon spoke out of him and said, “I am not leaving this strong body.  I 
am not coming out.” I said, “In the name of Jesus Christ come out of the man.” But the demon never did. I 
was ashamed and said, “This kind goeth not out but by prayer and fasting.” I was trying to cast out a devil 
from a sister and the spirit in her was so strong that about four of us had to hold her down. She held on 
to my tie and began to choke me. I escaped by the grace of God. 
I had the chance to see and hear the renowned “Apostle” Arturo Skinner, a preacher.  The same sister 
from Tobago, who had those demons in the form of pigs, having heard of the reputation of Skinner, came to 
Trinidad. I thought, “We would have the victory.”  
One Sunday, at the Queen’s Hall, the “apostle” was having a meeting and after preaching they brought 
the sister for the “apostle” to cast the devils out. He was dressed all in white and said, “Ocaparshanda.” 
It was an unknown language.  He made a lot of noise for about fifteen minutes or more, and commanded the 
devils to come out, but nothing happened, they refused to leave. Then he said, “This kind goeth not out but 
by prayer and fasting”, which we were doing all the time. I did not know that Skinner was an impersonator 
[px]of Brother Branham, until I came to the message of the hour. But there were also impersonators of Skinner; 
an evangeline (woman evangelist) and two evangelists who are now “apostles” and “doctors of divinity.” 
The evangeline was at the church trying to discern the people. I was next in line to a woman who 
looked as though she was with child and nearing time to deliver. I heard the evangeline tell the woman that 
she was having problems with the child she is pregnant with. The woman answered, “No lady, I am not 
pregnant.” Being next in line, all the faith I had just left. On another occasion a man was told that he 
could not eat because of a stomach problem; the man replied, “Not so, I can eat anything, even you.” One of 
the evangelists went three places and made a guess about a woman, whose name he said was, “Lois or Loicy or 
Loiceana.” No one showed up and at each place and on each occasion he said, “The spirit is crossed.”  
Then there was this brother who was filled with the Holy Ghost. Sometimes different parts of his body 
would twitch and vibrate and he would say that it is the Holy Spirit and he would pray until all his 
clothes would be wet. He ended up in prison for having sex with his daughter.  
[px]MIXED-BATHING – THE WORLD CREPT IN 
[px]Brothers and sisters go be mixed-bathing at the beaches. Sisters in their bikinis and brothers in their shorts. Though I was involved, I felt as a hypocrite, 
for we preached against women wearing pants, and that they should dress modestly, but here we allowed them to be almost naked in public and said nothing 
against it. To me, we strained at a gnat and swallowed a camel. We did not go to the Carnival but we enjoyed the ―parade of the bands‖ and the shows at home on 
the television. The music of the world crept into the church with the fashions and there was competition in dressing. The women cut their hair and used a hat or 
some kind of cloth to cover their head. I am not saying these things to be nasty. There are people, as far as I know, 
lived a holy life, even more than I. But I just want to bear witness to the truth that the Holy Spirit that 
we received was only an anointing on the flesh and on the spirit of man, but never touched the soul to 
change that evil nature.  
I came to find out about water baptism in the name of Jesus Christ and women preaching, and took a 
stand, and was therefore forbidden to preach or take any active part in the church. One year after, the 
“apostle” of the church sent for me. I went and he told me that he wanted to hand over the church to me 
because he knew where he stood with me. I told him that I could not accept his offer because I believed in 
baptism in the name of Jesus Christ, and that a woman should not preach and he did not believe those 
things. I told him that two cannot walk together except they agree. He said, “This is why I called you 
because you are the only one that I could trust. The others are underneath but I  
know where I stand with you.”  
On one occasion, I was preaching at another minister’s church. The other ministers used to sit behind 
the minister on the pulpit. While preaching against certain wrongs that were taking place in his church, 
[px]the pastor there started to pull on my jacket to indicate to me that I should stop what I was doing. The 
more he pulled on my jacket, was the more I preached against the wrongs in his church. 
Then, I was exposed to some of the books of the prophet. I wanted to come to the message but I did 
not want to be identified with the shame brought on by a backslidden preacher of the message who came from 
Pentecost with the baptism but his nature never was changed.  
I went to the “Church of our Lord Jesus Christ” where I met an old friend known as Brother Jackson. 
We spent some time there and stood for the teachings of Brother Branham, and they could not prevail over 
us. One of the deacons got in a rage in a meeting and said, “Chandler, I hate you! I don’t like your head!” 
The bishop of the church, in that meeting, had to agree with us that the evidence of the Holy Spirit was not speaking in tongues. He said that he believed Brother 
Branham was a prophet but he died. I asked him, ―What about his message?‖ He could not answer. Brother Jackson left and I continued 
there as pastor of the church in D’abadie and preached on the radio. By this time I was convinced that the 
Holy Spirit that was in me was not working as it did in the apostles.  
I baptized myself several times trying to master my evil nature. Oh what a wretched man I was. On the outside 
professing to be a Christian, but on the inside a prisoner to sin, a sheep on the outside but on the inside a ravenous wolf; reformed, but my soul was never 
touched. I changed the dressing of the women, but some rebelled.  
[px]FOUND THE TRUE MESSAGE OF THE BAPTISM OF  
THE HOLY GHOST 
[px]I was just about to be ordained as pastor, when I received an invitation from Brother Jackson who 
told me that God was using a brother in the message of the hour, not with signs and wonders but with the 
Word. I was so excited. I told Brother Barrow and another brother, and we came to Bethel. We did not get to 
hear Brother Bruce, but he sent two of his taped messages to me by Brother Jackson, something that he never 
did. My God, when I heard those messages, “Empty vessels” and “Seeking the kingdom”, that settled it for 
me. I told the church, “I have found a man with the true message of the baptism of the Holy Ghost.” I wrote 
a letter of resignation and told my wife, “I have found the thing I had been searching for all the days of 
my life. If you want to follow me, as Sarah did Abraham, you are free to do so. But, as for me, I am going 
to follow this message.” This was around September of 1977.  
Brother Bruce asked me to go and visit the church that I pastored, so he and I went. On arrival they greeted us with open arms and asked me to preach. I 
told them that I was no longer preaching, as I said before I left. I told them about Brother Bruce and they asked him to preach. It was the hottest message they 
ever heard. He did not spare anyone. After the message, they said that they were blessed and wanted him to come again. 
About two nights after, I took Brother Bruce to the head church and the bishop was preaching. When he saw us he stopped his message and greeted us. 
He said that he was one of us and he believed the message of Brother Branham and that he is in possession of the books. At this point in time he had no 
[px]knowledge that we had been to the other church and Brother Bruce had preached there. He hugged us and shook our hands. Days after, he found out that we were 
there and all the ―brotherly love‖ turned into wrath. He wrote me a letter in red and charged us not to step our feet on that compound or he would take us to 
court. I showed the letter to Brother Bruce. He read the letter and said, ―The blood of those people shall be upon the bishop‘s hand.‖ 
I sat down in Bethel and never did preach until five years after. I am now  assistant pastor of 
Guayaguayare, South Trinidad, also a deacon and a songleader. The Lord has used me to minister to some of 
the churches in the Caribbean and in the U.S.A. I am under the tutorship of Brother Dalton Bruce, whose 
life is blameless before all. A man who lives what he teaches. One of the only preachers in the entire 
world who gives to the people while others beg the people. The people are not perfect but are striving 
towards perfection. Our doctrine has never changed and will never change. Thank God, for our ministers are 
of one mind, humble and are against pride. I have fully given over my spirit to the truth of God’s Word 
under Brother Dalton Bruce. I am now contending for the true Pentecost, and challenge both the living and 
the dead, and none can stand and prevail against this because it is God. 
The whole creation is groaning until now, waiting for this outpouring. It would take a whole book to 
tell of all my experiences, good or bad. I thank the Lord Jesus for bringing me out of the Laodicean 
condition I was in. Praise be to God for his message by the seventh angel messenger to the churches in 
Laodicea. Thank God for a gift in this hour, for bringing me out of Pentecostalism and taking it out of me. 
These things are true. I am a witness and I am contending for the outpouring of the Spirit.  
The message of “One hundred years of Pentecostalism”, preached by Brother Dalton Bruce is the most 
powerful message I have ever heard. The clarity and dividing line, the exposition of Lucifer to Satan, in 
my own life; even the place that Judas sat in and still was lost. I never saw the children of Israel in the 
book of Corinthians or the Azusa Street experience. I have heard many messages preached by Brother Bruce, 
but this was a collection of them all. There was such an anointing and simplicity and revelation that left 
no room for unbelief. To me it is the message of the kingdom, and this same message, if never preach again, 
shall lead us into the great outpouring.  
This, to me, is what I call the headstone message. It has revealed my  
darkness, wretchedness, nakedness, blindness, poverty, and the miserable person I was, though religious. 
Surely it is the goodness of the Lord that has led us to repentance. It is as though I am being born all 
over again by the incorruptible seed Word from the opening of the seals, and now, obedience of faith is 
leading me to the token.  
There is enough power behind these messages to raise all the dead from the grave. I testify that all 
the ministers that have submitted themselves unto this ministry under Brother Bruce, are all of one mind, 
they are not after money, women or popularity, but are set for the defence of this token message.  
Our assistant pastor, Sookdeo Ramnarine, is a very humble man indeed. Pastor of Grace Covenant Church 
in the U.S.A,  Brother Kenny Mcgahee, is also a very humble person. I greet you all in India, Africa, 
Europe, Philippines, South America, the Caribbean, and wherever God’s children are. Brother Bruce has never 
changed his doctrine. At no time had he gone into fanaticism. He has always stayed in the middle of the 
road running a straight line.  
[px]There is a being that is connected to Brother Bruce that loves me very much. I can sing all the 
songs. I am so revived; a love spirit is around me to love all. Whatever I do it is from the inspiration of 
this message. It has lifted me up to a place that I have no desire to leave except it is to go higher. 
Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.  
Let us pray. Oh Lord, Father God, the voices of innumerable company of angels cannot express our 
gratitude. All that we are, we owe it all to Thee. Lord Jesus, thank you for this great deliverance. Oh 
Lord, I bear witness that your Word is truth, the whole truth and nothing but the truth. I bear witness 
that this revelation shall go into all the world and catch the elect in this end time here. I thank you for 
the mercy that has come along with this exercise, Lord. Oh God, for truly, by mercy and truth iniquity is 
purged and by the fear of the Lord men depart from evil. Thank you for thy servant, Brother Bruce; your 
goodness and mercy bottled up in the simplicity of human flesh. Thank you Father for the Word you have put 
into his heart that he may speak it unto us, Lord, for without that gift we could not even hear. “How can 
we hear without a preacher?” We thank you for him. We ask you to bless him; yea, we put all our faith 
together, all our strength, all of our minds. Anoint your gift, anoint your servant, protect him Lord and 
use him mightily, use him to shake down the kingdom of Satan. Lord, already I can see the impact of these 
messages out there, much more when they are anointed by the true outpouring of the Holy Ghost. I believe 
this will be a forerunner.  
All I want to do is sing and shout and dance and praise the Lord. It has put life into my soul; it 
has rekindled my zeal in me as when I first started out under Pentecostalism. Lord, I feel I can run with 
the footmen, the horsemen and with the swelling of Jordan, as if I can run through a troop and take the 
land. I say, “We are more than able; let us go up at once and take the land.” Forgive me, if I have over-
expressed myself, Lord, but this is just bubbling in me. I pray that the same inspiration on me would pass 
on to others. Much has been forgiven and we shall love much. Thank you for this great deliverance. May you 
guide and protect your people from all evil that shall come against us because of these messages. I know it 
shall do damage to the kingdom of Satan and he would not like that, but you are greater than the devil, 
Lord, and shall lift up a standard for us. I commit all things into thy hands, in Jesus precious name. 
Amen. 
[px]SISTER MYRNA BOXHILL - WIDOW 
[px]I was a witness of these things from a very young child. I grew up in Pentecost and I was sincere as a child. I believed in the baptism of the Holy Ghost, 
however, I was very confused about the behavioural pattern of those who claimed they had the baptism of the Holy Ghost.  
[px]PEOPLE MANIFESTED GIFTS OF THE SPIRIT YET THEIR LIVES WERE NOT RIGHT 
[px] I knew of people who spoke in tongues yet their lives were not straight. I knew of others who spoke in tongues, had the manifestation of different gifts 
of the Spirit and were living very clean lives. I knew of one woman who acted very strange when she said the Holy Ghost came upon her. Her voice was 
distorted as that of a man. She would disturb the song service, singing very loudly and out of chord. She did not use a microphone yet people would hear her 
from a distance. She had a very strange tongue which made me wonder as a youth. One night we had a prayer meeting and this woman started acting very 
strange. My mother, who was fasting at the time, drew closer to this woman and started praying for her. The next thing we knew is that the woman was stretching 
out on the ground and the spirit started speaking out of her in a man‘s voice saying, ―My time is expired‖. My mother started praying more, realising that it was 
not the Holy Spirit. The woman, by this time, was so distorted in her face, that her eyes were bulging out of her head. She took out her wedding band and said, 
―Give this to my husband.‖ She felt she was going to die. 
With much prayer that night the entire church bound themselves together and insisted that the demon had to leave this woman. The battle was fierce and 
long. As children, we were very fearful. It was an awesome experience. For days, this woman was very sick, until supernaturally the hand of the Lord did 
intervene and helped the woman. I saw others climbed on chairs hysterically, supposing to be filled with the Holy Spirit. Others lying on the ground, supposedly 
filled with the Holy Spirit, their wigs flying off their heads, women running with sheets to cover their nakedness. Then I thought to myself, ―I would like to have 
to the Holy Ghost but not in that fashion.‖ I did not want to blaspheme against the Holy Ghost but I was very troubled in my mind and I knew some were 
genuine, I knew some were demon possessed and I also knew some of them were working up themselves in a frenzy. So, I took a position where I did not doubt 
that there is the genuine anointing but I had problems with those who spoke in tongues and lived nothing.    
After a while I became very formal. I was a good observer and was afraid to yield myself to any spirit I was not sure about. Just then I felt very drained 
as if I had reached the end of my Christian walk, but I knew I did not reach my final destination and I did not know where to turn.   I stopped going to church as 
regular  as  I  used  to  go  because  from  the  pulpit  to  the  pew  needed  a  revival.  As  God  had  it,  I  changed  residence  and  came  in  contact  with  my  precious  Sister 
Cindy who has gone on. She invited me to Bethel (The House of God).  I heard things I had not heard before, yet something within was saying, ―I believe this is 
the truth.‖ I understood quickly  what  was required. I had to make  my decision. The price was heavy and hard, however, His grace  is  sufficient  for  me.  I 
understood clearly now what organisation had and still has, what the early church had on the day of Pentecost and what we are now looking forward to.   As the 
prophet said, "The Token or Perish", I now press towards the mark of the high calling in Christ Jesus.   
[px]SAMUEL JONES – MINISTER & RETIRED  
ESTATE SEARGENT 
[px]I am from Bethel, Trinidad. I grew up under Baptist parentage. Having no choice, I went along with my parents in obedience. As I grew older and read the 
Bible,  I  found  out  that  practices  of  "The  Faith",  as  it  was  termed,  were  contrary  to  what  I  read.  I  then  questioned  the  immoral  lifestyles  of  fornication  and 
adultery  of  some  of  the  leaders  and  members  (a  condition  I  had  left  in  the  world)  and  the  purpose  of  their  rituals.  This  caused  disturbances  which  resulted  in 
many heated debates, for a long time. 
Around that time, we  met and joined with some other Baptist brethren, who were having similar problems. With the quest for truth, we all got together 
and formed a study group, and assumed a name: "Free Baptist." We met a Preacher from South Trinidad, by the name of Dr. Peter Francis, who had also come 
[px]out  from  the  Spiritual  Baptist  faith  and  became  a  Full  Gospel  Minister.  This  association  took  us  to  several  Full  Gospel  Churches  throughout  the  country.  We 
grew in numbers and established a Fellowship, with Brother Errol Edwards as the pastor.  
Some time later, Brother Errol was introduced to ―Apostle‖ Baird of Faith Revival Ministries, who is a renowned Bible Teacher in this country. He advised 
us to attend an "Advanced Ministry Class" for two years at his Assembly for a better Pentecostal understanding. Brother Edwards, Brother Aldwyn James and I 
complied, from which we attained Certificates from that exercise.  
[px]THEY TAUGHT US HOW TO RECEIVE THE HOLY GHOST 
[px]Out of this association we had Ministers from Faith Revival visiting us from time to time who taught us how to receive the Holy Ghost, and after tarrying 
with us, some of the Brethren received it, and spoke in tongues. This, we believed then, was "the initial evidence". 
We then registered under the name of CHRIST GOSPEL ASSEMBLY LTD. at Montrose Village, Chaguanas, in Central Trinidad, with ―Apostle‖ Baird 
being one of the Directors.  
We then came in contact with an Evangelist from New Jersey, U.S.A. by the name of Jacob James, who preached to us knowledge on the scriptures that 
we never heard before. This birthed much confidence in our hearts towards him, and because of this, we allowed him to have the oversight over our group when 
he was in Trinidad.  
My  wife  and I had the  opportunity to visit  his assembly in  New Jersey, U.S.A. in the  eighties,  where  he  showed us a  plot of land that  he  said  was once 
occupied by followers of Brother Branham, and when he was at my home I saw some Spoken Word Books in his Bible case. I later found out that the "Truths‖ 
by which he had influenced our minds were obtained from the messages of our prophet, William Marrion Branham, which he hybridized with Pentecostalism. 
Pastor James came to Trinidad and held some meetings, during which he rebaptized most of the believers, in the Name of Jesus Christ.  
A special "cottage meeting" was called for those who needed to be filled with the Holy Ghost. In that meeting he taught on the Holy Ghost baptism, which 
we  tarried  for,  under his watchful eyes. We were taught to say, ―Hallelujah! Hallelujah! Hallelujah! repeatedly‖, which we did. I experienced uttering  strange 
sounds, while the tutor complimented us, to continue, and prompted us and said, ―Give it sound! Give it sound! Speak no English!  Speak  no  English!  No 
English! No English!‖ In the space of half an hour everyone in the house was speaking in tongues and crying. Our voices then somehow were amplified by the 
tongues with much rejoicing. It was a wonderful experience. We were then assured that we had the baptism of the Holy Ghost, and we all believed that. 
We  were  advised by him that from then on  we  must continue  to speak our new  language  whenever  we  are  alone  in our cars,  wind up our  windows  and 
fellowship with the Spirit. 
Out  of  these  experiences,  Sisters  Jones  and  Daly  were  outstanding  in  speaking  in  tongues  and  interpretations,  respectively.  Brother  Daly  was  the  only 
brother in the assembly who never received the ―Holy Ghost‖. This got him frustrated that he almost backslid. Had  it  not  been  for  our  beloved  Pastor  Dalton 
Bruce, whom the Lord had sent our way, the brother would have no longer been with us. 
[px]DISSATISFACTION 
[px]After several years of praying and fasting with hope to see the reality of the power of God we read of in the Bible, we were dissatisfied and wondered what 
next must be done because we desired to go on. A deeper hunger and thirst stepped in, but was not fulfilled.  
On Pastor James return, I asked him, ―How is it that we who now have the Holy Ghost are unable to experience and to do the things the apostles did?‖ His 
answer was that ―After Jesus was baptized, He went into the wilderness, fasted, and then came out in the power of the Spirit.‖ This answer  brought  about  a 
greater desperation to further seek God. 
[px]About that same time Brother Errol preached to us that we ―have to make a turn.‖ Some time after a co-worker  named  Mungal  Ramnarine,  a  deacon  at 
bethel, extended an invitation to me to attend some special meetings. I attended those meetings and while the preacher, Brother Dalton Bruce, was delivering his 
message, he said, ―There are some people who jumped out from a denominational ship, jumped in a Pirogue and are going around in circles  with  no sense  of 
direction. 
Immediately, I knew that I was that man in the Pirogue. Something about the atmosphere in that place brought about a peace in me. Right there and then I 
concluded that I was not returning to my former Assembly, but in obedience, I went for a short while to encourage the brethren to come and hear this great truth. 
I also invited Pastor Edwards who gladly accepted the invitation and complied. After the meeting he expressed thankfulness and desired to have his congregation 
hear what he had heard.  
Around that same time, Pastor James from the U.S.A., surprisingly called informing us of an unexpected visit. We suspected that he was informed about 
our new move and wanted to stop it, but continued to attend the meetings. He was greatly disappointed, and had no other choice but to accompany us.  
On that particular night, the Lord had Pastor Dalton Bruce to speak about all the "ills" of Pentecostalism, which had the evangelist a nervous man on his 
seat. On our return home he lectured to me all through the night until the morning, trying to turn me away from what I had heard. When he realized that he was 
unable to do so, he cut short his visit. 
Before leaving he asked to speak to the believers, with departing words as "I am not a Prophet, but I am prophesying that this Fellowship would not be the 
same again, mark my words".  
[px]DELIVERANCE FROM RELIGIOUS DECEPTION 
[px]Brother Bruce came to our assembly at the request of our then Pastor Edwards, after the special meetings at Bethel, and ministered to the Saints for three 
services. He rightly divided the Word of truth, which mightily delivered us from religious deception. The Word we heard from Pastor Bruce stripped us of all we 
thought we had known. 
Ninety Percent of the congregation went over to fellowship at Bethel and were rebaptized in the only form of Christian baptism, which is in the Name of 
the Lord Jesus Christ. After listening to Pastor Dalton Bruce‘s messages on ―Pentecostalism – One Hundred Years‖, where he stated that 1906 was a mixture of 
the genuine anointing and demon power, looking back at the spiritual  manifestations I had seen and experienced in both Baptist and so-called Pentecost, I was 
identified in that statement.  
Thank God who had given me ears to hear and a heart to believe  when the unadulterated Word was delivered to me. Amazing Grace  would always be 
my song of praise. My Pentecostal experience could not have qualified me for the rapture. I am sure that what I am now contending for is more than able to do 
so, as I must have the token or perish, according to the message of Malachi 4:5-6. Amen!   
[px]VINCENT JOSEPH – RETIRED ENGINEER 
[px]  I was born in the Catholic institution and could never understand what the priest was saying.  
   After I got  to the age  where  I felt I could now  chose  my  way of life,  the climax of dissatisfaction for the church ended on an Old Years  night. After 
hours of waiting for the priest to come to the altar, I saw some officers leading the priest through a back door into his presbytery. He was drunk so they had to 
lead him to the altar. I got up and never went back to that church. I was convinced that all along he had alcohol on his breath in the confession box. 
    I bought a Bible and read such words of comfort but I tried to join church by listening from the outside and watching the lives of their members.  
[px]     I  migrated  to  the  U.S.  in  1970  and  visited  my  two  sisters  in  Youngstown,  Ohio  where  I  entered  the  Pentecostal  church  my  sisters  attended.  I  heard 
speaking in tongues and saw most women and girls fell as they got what they called the ―Holy Ghost‖. The older women had sheets at hand to cover their legs on 
the floor.  
    Pastor Wagner was very vibrant and highly articulated. His congregation had about fifteen hundred people and his emphasis was on baptism in Jesus‘ 
name and speaking in tongues if you are to go in the rapture. He also preached that women should not wear britches which are pants, but they wore  
coulettes which is a pants-dress, that has legs. 
    I asked questions about the receiving of the ―Holy Ghost‖ and was told that I must be baptized in Jesus name and then go up to the upper room in the 
church.  
I was baptized and after much prayer and fasting I did not receive the ―Holy Ghost.‖ There were three sisters in the room to witness you receiving the 
―Holy Ghost‖. It took a long time exercising the speaking of the word ―Halleluiah‖ quickly. Months went by as I tried to get this gift and could not get it. After 
this, I then tried saying, ―Ahleluiah‖ and I still did not receive it, until one night while I was chanting ―Halleluiah, Halleluiah, Halleluiah‖, and the Halleluiah 
turned into something else. I had finally received it. So the church rejoiced and everyone was happy.  
[px]TONGUES COPIED AND IMITATED 
[px]I felt renewed, but after a  while I noticed that all the  ministers  were  speaking the  same  words the  chief Pastor Wagner  was saying. They began  their 
preaching most of the time with the words ―AH-SHAT-TA-MOYA‖, but no one knew the meaning.  
    I had some personal experiences on business matters with Pastor Wagner. He had intended to erect a school and office building. I was asked to help with 
this project because of my knowledge in construction and engineering.  During the course of the construction of the building, I was asked to order some building 
material on my company‘s name. I did so without questioning the method of refund of cash for the bills. After awhile, I approached Pastor Wagner on the bills 
incurred on my company‘s name. He sent me to the church treasurer who was also a preacher. The treasurer then sent me to a deacon and after much run around I 
had no success. The bills began to pile up until one of the lumber stores called me in and asked, why weren‘t my bills paid. I  then  told  them  I  had  taken the 
lumber for Mt. Calvary Pentecostal Church. They then told me that they too had been having problems for payment from the same church and as a result many 
other stores had them on their records as bad creditors. This is when I saw how dishonest a man who preached God could be. I had given thousands of dollars in 
job tithes, even before I had completed a job. 
    I  had  a  treasurer  minister  who  took  care  of  my  accounts  for  the  business  and  to  write  the  checks.  In  preparing  the  pay  sheets for  my  office  staff  and 
tradesmen he always showed deductions for taxes, but the state never received it, as I found out later. I knew then I had to get out of this mess.  
[px]IMMORAL, SPEAKING IN TONGUES & SINGING IN THE CHOIR 
[px]Our choir director invited me over to her apartment telling me that  
other brethren would be there also. I knew how she strong she stood for holiness, preached, spoke in tongues and was well respected. She also had her 
doctorate in music. I decided to go a little late and when I got there, there was no one else there but me. I asked, ―Where are the others?‖ and she replied, ―They 
will be here soon.‖  The table was all laid. I realized it was a trick when she went and changed her clothing so I then got up and started for the door when she 
blocked  me and told me, ―We are adults.‖ I had to then push my way through to escape that trap. The next day she was shamelessly leading the  choir,  and 
speaking in tongues as usual. I did have a few other encounters like that one but thank God he saw me through. 
[px]   Recently, I visited Youngstown, Ohio and was shocked to see the pastor‘s daughter in shorts in a fund raising meeting. He has lost all his credibility in 
Ohio and even the three-floor school building and offices due to his dishonesty with man and God.   
    I want to thank God for delivering me from Pentecost, opening my eyes to truth and bringing me to where it is being preached. Thank God for an 
honest and true minister of God and for his imparting the knowledge on how to receive the true baptism of the Holy Ghost. I know there are many sincere people 
in Pentecost and pray that they too will be delivered from the many deceptions that have them bounded. 
[px]KEITH BACHAN – MINISTER & HEALTH WORKER 
[px]  I am from Grace Covenant Church at Point Fortin, Trinidad. I came to the message in 1974. I started preaching in 1985, when the pastor fell in adultery.  
 We  were  already  aligned  to  the  New  York  Christian  Assembly,  therefore,  when  I  took  over  I  continued  that  trend.  I  attended  the  dedication  and 
convention of Pastor Wesco‘s church in West Palm Beach, Florida in 1985 and Pastor Coleman‘s convention in Penharris, Pennsylvania in 1986. 
 I bring to you the testimony of a true witness of what took place. 
[px]FALSE THUNDERS SEALING-HEADSTONE REVIVAL 
[px] There was a ―sealing revival‖ in progress. We had been receiving videos from the New York church on a regular basis; therefore, we were updated on 
all the activities that were taking place, whether in Puerto Rico, South Africa, Leon, France, and the conventions held before. 
 The theme of the convention was ―Charity, the headstone is here.‖ There was a big banner with the words, ―Charity, the headstone is here.‖ 
 The songs were sung in a Pentecostal rhythm and flavour. The New  
York church sings very fast and just like Pentecostals, they seem to enjoy songs that are very fast and have a lot of action. At that time African and Spanish songs 
were very popular among them. They considered themselves a church of action. Some of the songs had nice meanings and most were sung in an upbeat tempo. 
 The New York church is a very noisy church. They had found a quotation from the message which said that the Pentecostal church was a noisy church. 
They majored on quoting it often. 
  The  church  in  New  York  has  an  attendance  of  about  seven  hundred  members.  They  operate  a  shift  system  to  accommodate  the  members.  Pastor 
Coleman preaches in the morning and the evening shift looks at the video. 
 In the convention, the auditorium was big enough to hold everybody. There  was plenty of noise and wild worship. There is a conception among them 
that the more noise you have is more of the Spirit there is in the building. Many a times it is not.  
[px]PENTECOSTALISM UNDER THE MESSAGE 
[px] After  the  song  service  and  preaching,  there were  long  lines  formed  to  receive  the  seal  of  God.  In  the  convention  that  year  they  were  preaching 
―Pentecost has returned and we are in a sealing revival. Charity the headstone is here.‖ Not coming but ―is here, sealing the believers with the true baptism of 
the Holy Ghost.‖ 
[px] People seemed to program themselves to fall when hands were laid on them. You were told that when you pass through the line and hands were laid on 
you, you will be slain when receiving the seal of God. I wanted that thing so badly that I programmed myself. I knew I was going to fall before I got there. When 
I finally reached the minister who was laying on hands, his hands barely touched me and I was on the ground. I was glad about that because they were preaching 
that if you were no slain, something was wrong in your life. Therefore, everybody seemed to be receiving the seal of God: little children, three years old, middle 
age and old people. We really thought we were in a revival. 
 Just  as  you  have  heard  on  these  messages,  there  was  a lot  of  confusion  and  conflicts  taking  place;  those  who  fell  on  the  ground  were  uncontrollable. 
Sometimes women and men fell upon each other, rolling over each other etc. The helpers with sheets were kept busy, sometimes unable to cope with the amount 
of brethren on the ground. 
 I saw a lot of accidents take place, as women clashed with men on the ground: glasses were broken, people‘s clothing were ripped. A sister fell on the 
guitar and amplifier and damaged her back. Another sister who was a helper with the sheets said that a sister with a high heel damaged her foot. 
 Sometimes things got totally out of order. The people were so hard to
