Laodicea. 
 
 
MIDDLE LINE IN WORSHIP 
 
Let me put a little balance to the topic. I hope and trust that this does not lay a foundation for everybody to 
think that they are quiet in their nature and refuse to sing, refuse to  worship and refuse to thank God the way how 
God  designed  them,  when  they  come  to  the  House  of  the  Lord. The  very  same  people  who  think  they  are  quiet in 
nature, have the biggest mouth when the service is over, but cannot open their mouths to worship and praise God.  
It was reported that one young man went to a party and he drank a few beers and danced. This fellow who 
grew up in the House of the Lord was asked: ―Did you ever dance for the Lord?‖ He said, ―No.‖ He never danced 
for the Lord, but went and danced for the Devil. Do you see how deceitful people could become?  
The same quiet, pious and sanctimonious people that can‘t sing, can‘t open there mouths and praise God, 
see how excited they get with some kind of worldly thing or see how they behave when somebody gives a joke. You 
can hear them around  the  block.  Inside  the House  of  God  they  are  quiet but  outside  they  are  loud.  These  are the 
same  people  who  were  sitting  in  the  House  of  God  and  could  not  tell  God  thanks  for  the  food  they  eat,  for  the 
clothes they  wear and for the shelter over their heads. They could not sing in church, and if asked, they  would tell 
you that they can‘t sing, but they would sing worldly songs. This is deceit and it is hypocrisy. 
A  foolish  minister would  come  upon  the  pulpit  and  command  them  to  praise  God. And  if  he  beats  them 
hard  enough,  they  would  do  it,  but  while  they  praise  God  with  their  lips,  there  is  deceit,  hypocrisy  and 

unthankfulness  in  their  hearts.  They  have  no  life,  no  born  again  experience  and  they  are  plumb  out  of  the  Spirit. 
What good is it to them? It will serve them no good, for their hearts are not in it. 
If  you  beat  these  people  into  subjection  to  praise  God,  what  you  do  is  that  you  breed  ninety  percent 
hypocrites and you have trained them to say, ―Thank you Jesus, praise the Lord.‖ That is not true worship, and it is 
unacceptable to the Lord. It is vain worship. 
Ministers  in  this  message  are  wrong.  They  have  brought  over  their  so-called  Pentecostal  hogwash  habits 
under  the  message  and  are  trying  to  force  the  people  into  praising  God.  It  was reported that a man‘s wife and 
children under the message were disciplined for not praising God as they do.  
 The  Liberty one  could  exercise  on  the  pulpit  is  to  ask  the  congregation  to  sing  and  worship  the  Lord 
according to the leading of the Spirit. If you do not have the spiritual discernment when to do it and how to do it, to 
be on the safe side, it is better not to do it or leave such things alone, go on and sing your song, pick up the offering, 
do what you are supposed to do and step down, otherwise you could exercise some formality, and you will be told of 
it at Bethel. 
 If you meet a trained congregation you could come up to the pulpit and say, ―Everybody raise your hands 
and praise the Lord.‖ You could get a shocker of your life and nobody does it. Your call to worship ends up in you 
alone saying, ―Praise the Lord, thank you  Jesus‖, and to avoid the embarrassment and to save  your face  you 
encourage the congregation to say it also.  
 Spiritual discernment is very important in doing anything in the House of Lord. Our song leaders are told 
that they could ask the congregation at different intervals to say, ―Let‘s stand and sing a song or let‘s raise our hands 
and praise the Lord‖, but this must not take on a form and think that this must be done all the time. It does not work 
that way. The truth of the matter is that the congregation does not have that confidence in a song leader to obey him, 
as they would obey the pastor. 
If our Shepherd comes up here and says, ―Everybody lift your hands and worship the Lord.‖ Before he 
finishes the sentence, everybody‘s hands are lifted because they heard a voice of authority and they have utmost 
confidence in the man. Another person will come and say the same but does not get the response or co-operation. So 
the best thing to do, except you feel definitely led of the Lord, is don‟t do it. 
A minister could come and say, ―Let us worship the Lord‖, but a  
worship spirit is not there, and the right spiritual atmosphere or environment is not there. The people could only do 
that sincerely if a spirit of worship is there, and if it is, you hardly have to ask the people to do so. Spiritual worship 
would just flow. It will be automatic. It will be sweet and easy and they wouldn‘t want to stop.  
 Worship means that you are blessed. It means gratitude is coming from the heart. Spiritual worship makes 
you  feel  like  getting  up  and running. It makes  you  dance, it  makes  you  cry,  it  makes  you  sweet, and it makes  you 
calm and tranquilized. Spiritual worship leaves you changed in the spirit, and it leaves you more determined to serve 
your God. 
 On  another  occasion  the  congregation  is  bound  and  you  know  that  the  Spirit  of  the  Lord  is  trying  to 
breakthrough. You must have spiritual discernment to help the people. You may say, ―Let us all clap our hands and 
sing a song.‖ The reason for that is to drive the devil away, and you may directly feel led to say, ―Let us give the 
Lord a praise offering now‖, and with this you loose the service. But the truth is that everybody doesn‘t have that 
discernment  and  the  ability to  do  that. If  you  do  not  have  the  discernment  and  the ability  and  the  leading  of  the 
Spirit to do these things, then forget it. Do what you have to do and leave the pulpit. 
 
 
 Question # 8: ―In tarrying for the Holy Ghost meetings, is it scriptural for women to be exposed under the 
power of God?‖ 
 
E.O.D.H. Answer: In so-called Pentecost they have what you call ―Tarrying for the Holy Ghost.‖ When, 
what they call the ―Holy Ghost‖, comes upon the women, it causes them to fall on the floor and  expose  their 
underclothing. We hate it and God likewise hates it.  It is the deeds of the Nicolaitans, which God said He hates.  
Revelation 2:6 <But this thou hast, that thou hatest the deeds of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.> 
There  are  certain  senior  men  and  women  with sheets  to  cover  the  shame  of  the  nakedness of  those 
women when they fall, and to my greatest alarm they have brought this practise under this Holy Message. 
 So-called seven thunders have produced that practise under Coleman in New York, and right now it is still 
going on and they said that a revival has broken in New York. It is emotionalism, it is a frenzy, it is demon power 
and it is fanaticism. This practice is rampant and it is prevalent in so-called Pentecost.  
 You  cannot  convince  me  that the Holy  Spirit  will  come  upon  a  woman  and  cause  her  to  rudely  and 
shamelessly expose her nakedness like that, when the same Holy Ghost that inspired holy men to write the scriptures 

said, ―Let your women adorn themselves in modest apparel.‖ It is not the Holy Ghost. It is demon possession. It is 
reported that a woman had this experience and she lifted her dress where she was uncovered and you could not have 
gotten her to pull  
down her dress. 
 Question # 9: ―Is the pastor or the deacons in charge of the functions of the church?‖ 
 
 E.O.D.H. Answer: Beloved, it is clear from all that we have said before that the pastor is overall in charge 
of all the function that goes on in the House of the Lord. He is in control of the music program, all specials and all 
singing,  in  a  revival  or  in  any  service  that  the  church  has.  The  pastor  gives  permission  to  say  who  should  sing  a 
special and whatever goes  on in the House of God. If somebody is  on the list to play  on a Sunday evening and he 
feels led of the Lord to change two brothers and put up two others, that must stand that way, and not a ―dog should 
bark.‖ That is how the House of God is run. No one has any right to fuss with the pastor‘s decision when he sets in 
order the music, the song leading, the deacons and trustees, where they are supposed to  be. The pastor controls all 
the activities in the House of God, and what he say goes. That settles it for me. Why fuss; it only goes to show that 
you have to have another spirit. 
Any pastor that will allow the people to have long drawn out testimonies to have their own way and kill the 
service, it is because he does not know his God given rights and he has the church running any old how.  If   in   a 
simple thing like this he does not know what to do, there is lot of other things that he does not know. When you have 
a man with a long drawn out testimony it bores the people, it kills the service, and by the time the preacher gets to 
the pulpit the people are tired and hanged down and cannot hear the most important part of the service. This reflects 
that you are not serious enough. That is Pentecostalism and it opens the door to bring in all kind of foul spirits.  
There is nothing wrong in giving a testimony of what the Lord has done for you. Your motive and objective 
must be right. It must be to glorify God, to convince sinners to be saved and backsliders to hold on to the Lord and 
not give up.  
 This problem happens mostly with those who want to show-off. If you give them fifteen minutes or half an 
hour  to  testify,  they want to  give  their whole  life  story  and harp  on  the  part  that  glorifies  themselves.  You  will  be 
blessed for telling the bad things about yourself. The bad things humble your heart.  
 Come  up  and  say  that  you  fret  and  fuss  and  you  cannot  settle  matters  peacefully and  you  have  made 
enemies among your brethren in the church, and ask the Church to forgive  you and pray for  you, and say that you 
will try your utmost best not to cause any problems again by fretting and fussing. 
 We here at Bethel have passed that hurdle many years ago, and the brethren that testify cut their testimony 
to suit or make it to the point. The brethren use the allotted time without being given a specific time limit. The truth 
is  that  you  have  one  or  two  brethren  who  still  bore  the  congregation.  After  they  talk  for  about  fifteen  to  twenty 
minutes, or half and hour, they forget what they were saying and would say. ―O, I can‘t remember right now. Any 
how, what I was saying is this.‖ That is not the way to testify.  You don‘t even have common sense to know that you 
are  boring  people.  You  must  use  common  sense  in  your  personal  conversation  and  speaking  to  members  of  the 
public to know when a man is tired of hearing you and he had enough and don‘t want to hear any more. You could 
meet  a  person  with a bad or forgetful memory and they could tell the same thing five times. ―Do you know that I 
caught a ten pound fish the other day?‖ And tomorrow when he meets you again says that he caught an eleven 
pound fish. The fish starts growing bigger and bigger.  
 The man don‘t want to embarrass you and tell you that you told him that same fish story a week ago, but he 
stops there and tells you, ―Yes‖, but the man wants to go. But before the man goes, the person says, ―Hear this, I 
caught a twelve pound fish.‖ He  has  a  bad  memory.  You  are a boring  fellow  in  talking  to  people.  No  one  would 
want to give  you a hearing ear. If  you are boring to  your co-worker, friends, relative, brothers and sisters, you  will 
be boring on the pulpit.  
 When you testify you must testify with a certain vision to convince the youths that they should buck up and 
the young ladies that you were young but you held on. You are trying to convince the old man to give his soul to the 
Lord before he departs this life. You are testifying to convince the visitor to accept the Lord as Saviour.  
You could have a fellow who comes up to the pulpit to boast. It would bring in the wrong spirit and grieve 
the Spirit of the Lord, and it doesn‘t pave the way for the minister to enter the pulpit and preach. People  are  not 
blessed by long drawn-out testimonies. They are bored and sick. 
 
 
 Question # 10: ―We know that a woman cannot preach (1 Timothy 2: 12) but can she be a writer of the 
message and gospel?‖ 
 

 E.O.D.H. Answer: No, she is forbidden to teach and usurp authority over the man (1 Timothy 2: 12). The 
first time she handled the Word in Genesis 3, Satan deceived her. She is commanded by the scriptures not to teach 
the Word in any form: preaching, teaching or writing. Both in the Old and New Testament there was not a woman 
writer,  teacher,  preacher  or  scribe.  This  example  in  scripture  is  sufficient  to  convince  any  Christian  that  it  is 
forbidden by the Word. 
Amongst the followers of the message, it is fully established that a woman is forbidden from teaching the 
scriptures.  Upon such grounds,  they  hold  their  scriptural  places  and  are  afraid  to  enter  the  pulpit  to:  lead  songs, 
introduce  preachers,  make  announcements,  read  the  scriptures  and  preach  the  Gospel. In  recent  years,  however, 
some have formulated ways and means by the help of sissified preachers, not born again nor sealed of God, but are 
mere womanizers, to allow women to exercise this forbidden authority from behind the curtains, by way of voicing 
their  opinions on  the  message,  scriptures,  church  order,  songs  and  music,  by  way  of  literature,  books,  magazines, 
Newsletters  and  their  photographs.  This  is  totally  unscriptural  and  cannot  be  found  in  the  Gospel  message.  Peter, 
Paul nor  John never  engaged  a  female  in  such  fashions, neither as  their  scribes. There  are  no  Epistles  in  the  New 
Testament written by a woman (acting as a scribe for Brother Paul), introducing him in his Epistles, on the pulpit, or 
voicing their opinion about his message in any fashion. It is contrary to the message.  
Brother  Branham never  tolerated  such things, nor had  any  of  his  written  work  completed  or  done  by  any 
female,  nor  ever  authorized  any  female  to  introduce  him  nor  his  message  at  his  Tabernacle,  on  the  radio  or  on 
television,  because  she  is  to  remain  silent  with  all  subjection  and  not  be  permitted  to  speak  (teach  or  preach)  as 
commanded  by  the  Lord  in l  Corinthians  14:34-37.  All  such  women  who  exercise  this  authority  from  behind  the 
curtains  are  strictly  out  of  their  scriptural  place  and  need  to  repent,  along  with  the  pastors  and  big  names  in  the 
message, for they are all blind guides leading the blind. 
We ask for one quotation to prove where Brother Branham indicated, suggested or authorized any female to 
exercise any such authority behind the pulpit, in literature or from behind the curtains. This is a woman preaching 
spirit, just as bad as any  woman behind the pulpit in so-called full gospel  churches, boasting about how many got 
saved  and  are  blessed  by  their  preaching,  writings,  tapes,  television  and radio  programs.  God  only  recognizes  His 
Word. 
Some ministers wives take the women aside and hold meetings with them, quoting Titus2:3-5 that the elder 
women should teach the younger women to love their husbands and so forth. But they are wrong.   
This  teaching  of  the  younger  women  by  the  older  women  is  limited  to  an exemplary  life  and  sound 
personal  advice,  and  has  nothing  to  do  with  congregational  gatherings of women  taught  by  pastors'  or  deacons' 
wives  or  any  super  spiritual  women,  for  they  cannot  do  so  without  breaking  down the  message  and  scriptures, 
throwing their hands forward, kicking their heels, rebuking someone and preaching the Gospel. 
Preachers  who  are not  called  of  God make  excuses  for  women  to  break  down  the  Word  from  behind  the 
curtains.  Preachers'  wives  are  afraid  to  enter the  pulpit  because  genuine  servants  of  God  who  preach  this  message 
and  uphold  the  Word  without  compromise  will  make  war  with  such  hypocritical  pastors  and  their  sissified  spirit. 
Thus the wife preaches her message to her husband from behind the curtain, in the secret chamber and he does her 
preaching from behind  the  pulpit,  kicking  his  heels,  pretending  to  discern  and  be  in  contact  with  the  heavenly 
throne,  inspired  by  the  Holy  Ghost,  while  his  wife  punctuates  her  own  message  with  a  masculine  "Amen! 
Hallelujah! Praise the Lord!" The result of such messages is confusion, because they are generated by an evil spirit, 
gossip, women confusion, backbiting, offences, enmity and hatred between pastors' families and church members.  
Women with  such preaching spirits cannot abide  under true servants  of God in the  message. When 
they find a preacher that's not called of God, not born again and without the Spirit of Christ, they make him 
a  woman's  puppet.  They  pastor  the  church  from  behind  the  scene.  Such  a  woman  is  hailed  to  be  super-
spiritual and one hearing from God.  
Question  # 11: “If a  pastor‘s wife  is  spiritual,  can she  give him Scriptures  to  preach,  and  should  he 
listen to her?” 
  
E.O.D.H. Answer: NO!!! If she was spiritual at all she won‟t do that. So she is really not spiritual. The 
Bible says:  
“Let your women keep silence in the churches: for it is not permitted unto them to speak; but they are 
commanded  to  be  under  obedience,  as  also  saith  the  law.  And  if  they  will  learn  any  thing,  let  them  ask  their 
husbands at home: for it is a shame for women to speak in the church. (1 Corinthians 14:34-35). 

 This Scripture is turned around: “Let her tell her husband at home.” If you start listening to her, 
taking the Scriptures that she wants you to preach, then you are letting her tell you at home. The Bible says, 
“Let her ask you at home.”  
Don‟t you realize that the first time that a woman tried to handle the Word, that she caused the fall 
in  the  Garden  of  Eden  and  she  brought  home  a  scripture  for  her  husband about  how  nice  the  fruit  tasted. 
When  she  brought  home  that  Scripture  it  destroyed  him  also?  She  mishandled  the  Word  and  the  fall  of  all 
humanity came because a man listened to the voice of his wife.  
<And unto Adam he said, because thou hast hearkened unto the voice of thy wife...> (Genesis 3:17).  
That woman is not supposed to open her mouth and give you one Scripture to preach, whether good 
or bad. That‟s not her place, that‟s a preaching spirit. The next thing is that she will tell you what to preach 
about.  
 
 
Question # 12 “Is a Divine Call To Preach The Gospel The “Ability” To Understand And “Speak” The 
Message: Also a Dream Or A Vision Of A Brother Preaching?” 
 
E.O.D.H. Answer: No. This is a false interpretation and is contrary to the Prophet‘s message of Malachi 
4:5, 1 Corinthians.2: 1-4. 
              Preaching is not how well you can speak, but preaching is whether the Spirit is attendant upon the Word or 
not.  
To  believe  that is  incorrect,  false  and  erroneous.  It is  the  spirit  of  Nicolaitanism (Revelation  2:6) that 
eventually invaded the pulpit with their errors and conquered the laity or church in the early Christian ages. It is the 
white horse rider and it is still conquering the souls of the people in religion and under the message. 
Exodus 3:1-10: Moses the Prophet was divinely called out  by a supernatural pillar of fire said: “Oh 
my Lord, I am not eloquent” (Exodus 4:10.)  
A divine call is one thing and an ability to speak is another. 
Our  Moses,  Brother  Branham  could  not  have  spoken  well; but  God  supernaturally  called  him  and 
used him, like he had never used any man (Outside of the Lord Jesus). 
Quote: „No man has a right to preach, less he like Moses meets him out there in that scared ground that 
pillar of fire hanging there, where he knows where he is at.‟ (Future Home 1964). 
 
False interpretation of a divine call could deceive and mislead God‟s children. That is where the spirit 
of antichrist begins to work, when he succeeds in getting or putting preachers without a divine call upon the 
pulpit, he will inject thoughts and ideas, errors and false interpretation in the message by  men not called to 
the  ministry.  They  will  make  themselves  antichrists  and  false  prophets  right  under  the  disguise  of  the 
message, though their intention be right, that Jesus so warned us about that will take place in the last days. 
(Matthew 24:11-24) (2 Corinthians 11:13-15).  
The Devil has a five-fold ministry. The devil sent them to create doctrinal confusion and battle in the 
message to deceive God‟s people right under the message. 
This  is  what  is responsible for  all  the  major  doctrinal  differences,  false  interpretations,  divisions, 
fighting,  schisms,  fussing,  confusions,  adulterous  preachers,  thieves  and  liars  in  the  message  is  because  the 
majority of men on the pulpit do not have a divine call to handle the Word.  
A man  must not enter the pulpit because he is ambitious and he likes it for a career. This is not the 
way  preaching  the  gospel  is  approached.  A  brother  must  not  make  himself  a  preacher  based  solely  on  a 
dream or a vision that he was preaching to a congregation. Dreams and visions do not qualify any brother to 
be  a  preacher.  For  a  brother  to  be  a  preacher,  he  must  fulfil  certain  basic  scriptural  requirements  to  be 
qualified as a preacher. 

 A preacher must be supernaturally called like Moses; Exodus: 3:10 and like Paul; Acts: 9: 15-16. He 
must  be  trained  in  the Word.    A  preacher  must  be  the  husband  of  one  wife  and  his  children,  once  they  are 
living in his house must be in the faith; 1 Timothy 3:1. 
 A preacher must be loyal to the Word, like the apostles, Acts: 1:2-42 and like Paul. 
If  you  are  a  preacher  and  you  have  not  fulfilled  these  requirements you  are not  qualified to  be  on  the 
pulpit. 
 
 
Question # 13: ―In our church, ministers are led by prophecies from the prophets; should they do that?‖  
 
E.O.D.H. Answer: Oh, you have prophets? A minister should be led only by the Word of God and by the 
Spirit of God and any prophecy that he obeys should dovetail with the Word of God and the message of the hour, 
and if a prophecy is contrary to the Word of God then that minister should not obey that prophecy. The first thing I 
would  ask  here, how  do  you  determine who  are  prophets  in  your  church?  There  is  a  difference  of  a  man 
prophesying and a prophet. A man who prophesies does not necessarily have to be a prophet.  
The second thing I will ask here, did these prophecies come in a scriptural manner? Did you have three 
spiritual men to judge the prophecy? Did  you have another spiritual man with the discernment of spirits to confirm 
whether that is a right spirit or a wrong spirit, before you accepted that? Did you put that prophecy under test of the 
word of God, before you as a minister obeyed that?   
 
YOU DON‟T EXAMINE YOUR PROPHECY WITH GOD‟S WORD 
 
Quote: 39-6    ―They  were  prophets, but if  they'd  have  stopped  and examined  their  prophecy with  the 
Word... They reasoned; they said, "That belongs to us, so we'll go up to Ramoth Gilead and we'll take it...That's 
what's the matter today. You don't examine your visions your prophecy and your doctrine with God's Word.‖ (Way 
of a true prophet 62-0513m). 
 
Now the devil could come into the church through prophecy, minister brothers, and sweeten you up by 
many  prophecies  and make you get a big “swollen head”, as Ahab‘s head was swollen through four  hundred 
prophets. An evil spirit from God came upon Ahab‘s prophets and they all prophesied that they should go to war, 
and will take Ramoth Gilead and have the victory, and there was only one prophet called Micaiah who was speaking 
by the Spirit of God.  
All these things are deceptions ministers. You‘ve got to watch for deception. Yes! Gifts that travelled 
down from 1906 is right under the message today, and in order to deceive backslidden preachers to go to battle, to 
fight  at  Ramoth  Gilead,  telling  them  that  they  are  going  to  win  thousands  of  souls.  Prophecies  could  come  to  the 
church and start swelling your head: ―My servant had spoken tonight; obey him or perish. I will lift him up and send 
him  around  the  world.  I  love  my  servant.‖  And  another  person prophesies  tomorrow night,  and  a  next  man 
prophesies  another  night,  and  another  fellow  prophesies  the  next  week,  and that  could  be  a  devil,  just  for  that 
minister to start thinking that he is someone great. And his shoulders start raising, and he goes around with a big 
chest and say, ―I have thus saith the Lord!‖ Where did you get your thus saith the Lord from? ―A Sister spoke last 
week. A Brother spoke last week!‖ Did you check that out?   
A next prophecy comes forth: ―Whosoever comes against my servant, I will cut them off.‖ Oh, he thinks he 
has great power. ―I will use him in a mighty way throughout the world.‖ The  devil  is  speaking in  the  church  to 
overthrow that minister with pride, ambition and self. Then, after a while, that same woman who  was prophesying 
falls in adultery, and that same man who was prophesying falls in adultery. Try the spirits.  ‖Prove all things; hold 
fast that which is good.‖ (1 Thessalonians 5:21). ―Beloved, believe not every spirit, but try the spirits whether they 
are of God.‖ (1 John 4:1).  
Then another sister says, ―Oh pastor, when I was sitting over there I saw a light over you.‖ That fellow gets 
blown up. He says, ―I have vindication from God. The Pillar of Fire is with me.‖ Watch out friends, a devil is setting 
a trap for you ministers. These things destroyed the 1906 movement. They are the foundation of Pentecostalism and 
now under the message.  
Now,  let  us  examine  these  things  that  I  just  said,  by  what Brother  Branham had  to  say  on  this  subject  of 
prophets and prophesy.  
 
YOU'RE MAKING PROPHETS OUT OF MEN THAT'S NOT PROPHETS 
 

   Quote: 63    ―Where you got off, was this, you're making prophets out of them men and things that's not 
prophets... Prophets are borned. See? In the Bible there's a gift of prophecy. There's where your  mistake  was 
between the gift of prophecy and a prophet... the Body of Christ has nine spiritual gifts operating in it. And it might 
be on this woman tonight, prophecy, may never be the rest of her life...That doesn't make no one a prophet. It's a gift 
of  prophecy in you. And before that...phecy  can  be  given  to  the  church,  it  has  to  be  judged  before  two  or  three 
spiritual judges...‖ (Demonology 2 Religious 53-0609). 
 
               Many ministers are being led by these ―prophets‖ of the church. God do not allow his gifted ministers to be 
dependant upon a gift of prophecy and a prophesier. He speaks to them directly and may confirm that revelation by 
other gifts. He does not have his servants as dumb driven cattles through the gifts of prophecy, or dream as they are 
secondary, uncertain and can fail, be wrong and deceptive. (I Corinthians 13:8). 
 
 
Question  # 14: ―Pastors are led by dreams and visions of believers; some ministries were confirmed by 
dreams. Some pastors dreamed that they are prophets, messengers to lead the bride. How do I know if they are true 
of false?‖  
 
E.O.D.H. Answer: Very simple. Go back to Deuteronomy 18: 22:  
―When a prophet speaketh in the name of the LORD, if the thing follow not, nor come to pass, that is the 
thing which the LORD hath not spoken, but the prophet hath spoken it presumptuously: thou shalt not be afraid of 
him.‖  
Deuteronomy 13:1-3 says that ―If he prophesies and the thing comes to pass, but then afterward he will say, 
let us go and serve other gods, I have not sent that prophet.‖ If he changes the Word of God, no matter what he says 
and it comes to pass like Balaam, God did not send him.  
Judas came from hell and went back to hell, but the apostolic gift in him was right; it came from God, in so 
much that God took the apostolic gift and gave it to Brother Paul. ―Let his bishopric be taken and given to another.‖ 
(Acts 1: 20).  
Now  see  what  that  apostolic  gift  done  to  the  gentiles.  That  is  what  it  was  supposed  to  accomplish  in  the 
man  called  Judas  if he had not  fallen  by  transgression.  A  man could  sell  out  his  gift  and  he  could  use  it  for  the 
devil.  
You could have a proper dream here tonight, and then, tomorrow, you can have a dream that is wrong. An 
evil spirit comes in and give you a dream. An evil spirit comes in there and deceives you.  
He gave us another warning: he said, “Leave those dreams alone if there is nobody to interpret it.”  
 
Quote W.M.B.: E-46 God can deal in dreams...but  they're  not  too  accurate,  especially  in  this  day. (Do 
You Now Believe?  52-0817).  
 Quote: E-8 If...any  dreamer  dreams  a  dream  that's contrary to This, then it's wrong...It  must  come  from 
the Word. I'm a great believer in the Word. That's what keeps us straightened out. Come back to the Word of God. 
(Mark Of Christ 55-0312). 
 
 
Question # 15: ―Some ministers and young men fasted long and prayed to have power like the prophet 
WMB, and lost their minds, others died. Are long fasts scriptural?‖  
 
E.O.D.H. Answer: Satan  misguided  such  sincere,  honest  and  desperate  souls. There  are no  Scriptures 
which  command  an individual;  minister,  lay-member,  male  or  female  to  have  long  fasts. Many  presume  that  they 
should  fast  for  twenty-one  days  or  forty  days,  because  they  read  in  the  scriptures,  about  Moses  and  Jesus  having 
fasted for forty days, or Daniel for twenty-one days. This is a heresy, which so-called Pentecost has propagated for 
many  years.  Many  lost their  minds  and  were  committed  to  insane  asylums.  Others  became demon  possessed  and 
some  died. We  cannot  do  God  a  service  without  his  will;  when  we  do  things  contrary  to God‘s will, plan and 
purpose, He is not responsible for us; but many times because of our ignorance, he has mercy upon us. Many books 
were  written  through  the  ignorance  of  authors  which  prompted  sincere  people  to  seek  God  for  power  to  do  signs, 
wonders  and miracles. One such book is ―Atomic power through fasting‖ by Franklin Hall. Many were deceived 
into  long  fasts  and  received  no  power  with  God  because  power  is  not  by  fasting  but  by  the  genuine  article  of 
Pentecost,  the  outpouring  of  the  Holy  Spirit. ―And ye  shall  receive  power  after  that  the  Holy  Ghost is  come  upon 
thee.‖ We  learnt  through  witnesses  that  Brother  Branham  preached  to  people  who  were  associated  with  Franklin 

Hall and his teachings.  He wisely attacked that heresy by saying that people asked him ―if he had many long fasts to 
have such great power with God‖, and he said to them ―I never fasted for more than 3-4 days in my life‖.  
 
THREE DAYS IS THE LONGEST I EVER FASTED IN MY LIFE 
 
 Quote: 12    ―A few weeks ago, there was a circular letter...said I had been with this person for thirty days 
on a fast. Three days is the longest I ever fasted in my life.” (Go wake Jesus 63-1130E).   
 
 This  so-called  Pentecostal  idea  and heresy  were  brought  over  under  the  message by  Pentecostal 
preachers who are ignorant of the truth concerning fasting. This Heresy is based upon the gross misrepresentation of 
the scriptures. We should take for an example the prophet of our age, William Branham Elijah the prophet, Malachi 
4:5-6.  He  never had  long  fasts,  though he  contemplated on doing so. He later called it ―nonsense‖, which he must 
―forget and move on with God‖.  
 
THERE'S NOTHING IN THE WORD TELLS YOU TO HAVE A FORTY DAY FAST 
 
Quote:  111e ―Forget it. Dump it out of your soul. Say, "God...I want to forget all this foolishness of all 
these forty day fasts and see if I can get closer to God, and all this, that, and the other, all of this nonsense." Stop it.    
There's nothing in the Word tells you to have a forty day fast, not a thing.‖ (Enticing spirits 55-0724). 
 
 God is not responsible for message ministers or believers, who presumptuously choose to go on long fasts, 
through their ignorance. Some are conscious of the lack of power and manifestations amongst message believers and 
have in the past fasted to bring that power into the church. They are ignorant of the fact that power is not by fasting, 
but by another outpouring of the Holy Spirit, like the day of Pentecost. In Central Trinidad a man died after a long 
fast. Later, a preacher friend of mines died the same way. He developed prostate cancer soon after he ended his long 
fast for approximately sixty days. The young man fasted at the old preacher‘s home and died. 
 
 
    Question # 16: ―How is a prayer meeting conducted? Some praying, beat and break the chairs, scream, 
swing their hands and hit others. Women take the lead.‖  
 
E.O.D.H. Answer: Many  who  came  out  of  so-called  Pentecost  are  acquainted  with  fanatical  prayer 
meetings.  Others  who  are not  familiar  with the  system  of  so-called  Pentecost  are  alarmed,  when  they  hear  of  such 
things. Some who embraced the message, brought over their Pentecostal traits of praying and doing many  other 
religious things, through their former training, and maybe are  yet ignorant of  what is true prayer and worship, and 
must be taught. This question reflects that fact. Some demonic activities are involved and much over-emotionalism. 
There  is  no  scripture  to  support  such  fanaticisms  of  breaking  chairs,  injuring  persons,  screaming  uncontrollably, 
women   unveiling   themselves,   speaking   in   tongues without   interpretation   and   many   other   unscriptural 
manifestations. 
Such manifestations are accepted by ministers of so-called Pentecost and now even by message ministers as 
the ―moving of the Spirit”, but it is not.  Its  fanaticism and heretical! Though  such  people  may be  sincere, honest 
and  desperate  for  God  and  to  have  their  prayers  answered,  they  are  untrained  and  out  of  scriptural  order.  The 
minister  himself  may  have  brought  over  such  Pentecostal  traits  under  the  message  and  his  church.  Thus,  such 
manifestations are commonly accepted by him and from his congregation.  
 Real  prayer  meetings  are  to  be  conducted  in  the  most simple  and  humble  manner! The  saints  of  God  are 
gathered,  music  can  be  played  to  invite  the  Spirit  of  the  Lord, and  some  singing  can  be included  to  get  the  people 
into the spirit. Psalms can also be read as a form of prayer, if the saints are not praying out to the Lord as expected. 
Certain persons can be appointed to lead in prayer one after the next without using the pulpit or having to call upon 
them Those who are not praying should try to yield to the spirit by repentance, confession of sins, lifting holy hands 
unto the Lord and agree with the prayer of  others who are praying! (Matthew 18:19). It is important to understand 
that all do not have the same nature and expressions to the Lord and may choose to pray in their hearts like Hannah, 
whose  lips  moved  and her  words  were  not  heard; insomuch  that Eli  judged  her as  a  drunken  woman.  Others  may 
weep  before the Lord silently and others openly. God deals with his subjects in the way that He designed them 
and one should not judge another, because they are not loud in their expressions!  
   
 
Question # 17: ―Should Christians be superstitious and visit obeah (witchcraft) men or witch doctors?‖   

 
E.O.D.H. Answer: There  are  many  Bible  believers  and  some  message  believers  who  are bound  by 
superstitions.  They  regard  omens,  superstitious  signs,  believes  in  many  old  wives  fables  and  mythical  stories  of 
Ghost  under  different  titles.  Many  of  those  believers  were once associated  with religions and  organizations,  which 
promote  such  superstitions.  Such  evil  travelled  down  from  old  pagan  religions,  Baalamism,  Babylonianism  and 
Romanism,  which  eventually  was heaped  up  in  Catholicism  and  branched  of  into  other organizations.  Seeds  of 
superstitions are sown into the tender hearts of children by superstitious parents and other people. They are moulded 
in  fear  and truly  believe such  superstitions  which  become part  of  their  daily  lives.  Such  seeds  are  in  people  when 
they become Christians.  
Superstitions which have their roots in pagan religions and mythology, have bound billions of people in the 
past, and most of the world is yet bound, bewitched, and live in fear and dread of evil forces. Their religious leaders 
sell certain prayers, candles, chains, rings, and millions of other items to such people, with the promise that they will 
be protected by them. Even some message believers visit these places in hope of finding deliverance from withcraft, 
obeah or some spell that was done to them by someone else. Christians are under the blood and no evil shall harm 
them if they exercise faith in Christ and the blood of the cross, and it is free.  
 
 
 Question # 18: ―For wedding ceremonies some women dress with high combed hairstyles; can‘t worship. 
Is this scriptural?‖ 
 
 E.O.D.H. Answer: In weddings or other functions, people who are not born again and filled with the Holy 
Spirit, are anointed by a spirit of society. They are changed automatically  from Christians to worldly persons, and 
endeavour to fit their functions into society  with pump and pride. Such ungodliness are expressed by the women, 
by semi-Christian dresses, shoes and hairstyles, change of accents,  in verbal addresses, insomuch that some of them 
are unrecognizable as the same people who come to church from service to service. Such practices are hypocritical 
and unscriptural. 
   
 
 Question # 19:  ―Are Sunday schools scriptural?‖ 
 
E.O.D.H.  Answer: No! The  prophet  of  God  said  it  is  not,  but  is a  Methodist  dogma that started  in 
organization. Nobody could tear that down with the Word. 
 
SUNDAY SCHOOL'S -THAT'S A METHODIST DOGMA. 
 
   Quote: 33-3 Each  church  trying  to  outdo  another  one  in  Sunday  school.  And Sunday  school's not  even 
spoke of in the Bible. That's a Methodist dogma. It was first called ragged school. Everybody now goes to Sunday 
school that can't stay for church, because they took something besides what God said. That's right. And remember, I 
never  said  I  preached anything  in inspiration in  my life  I  had  to  take back, like  I did the  altars  the other  day  and 
things, you know. Serpent's seed or any of that, let someone come and tear it down with the Word of God. (Sirs we 
would see Jesus  61-1224). 
 
 
Question # 20: “Message ministers have instituted additional offerings and pledges and pull for money 
just like so-called Pentecost. Is this scriptural?‖ 
 
E.O.D.H.  Answer: All  that the  scripture requires of  a believer  is  to  pay  their tithes,  offerings, and  in the 
Old  Testament  there  was  what  you  called  a  building  fund and  such  like.  All  else,  such  as  pledges, pastor‟s love 
offering,  begging  and  bumming  for  money,  collections  for  feasts  where  believers  must  pay  for  what they  eat  etc., 
are unscriptural and has there roots in Pentecostalism.  
A  money loving  spirit  is  a  Judas  spirit.  He  sold  the  Word for thirty pieces of silver. Preachers‘ wives 
around the message boast of millions of dollars in the bank, money pulled out of the people from around the world. 
They  and  their  families  live  high  and  luxuriously,  unlike  true  servants  of  God  who  they  and  their  families  live 
humble  lives,  share the  tithes and  offerings, and  even their personal  wealth  with the  believers. That  is the  spirit  of 
Balaam  the  false  prophet, upon  these  money  loving  preachers, which  ended  up  in  organization and  the  Catholic 
System. Brother  Branham never  pulled his  congregation  for  money,  and no  true  preacher will,  but  like  Samuel he 
will set the right example. 

 
I DON’T BELIEVE IN PULLING, BEGGING AND BUMMING MONEY 
 
Quote W.M.B.: Brother Branham is it wrong for ministers to make long pulls for money in their services, 
telling that God has told them that so many in the audience are supposed to give so much? If this is right, I want to 
know. Or if it is wrong, I want to know. This had disturbed me terribly?  
I  think it‘s terrible...I  do  not  believe  in  pulling  and begging  and  bumming  money. I think it‘s a wrong 
thing.... I do not believe in it I‘ve even knowed of Ministers at a big convention- took the whole afternoon, standing 
on the platform and threatening that God would destroy their crops, would give their children polio, and things like 
that, if they didn‘t put into that meeting... It‘s blasphemy to God and to his followers. (Question & Answers on the 
Holy Ghost 19/12/59 Pg. 21). 
 
 
Question  # 21: ―Is it scriptural to claim your neighbour‘s goods in the name of the Lord? Is  this  not  a 
covetous spirit?‖ 
 
E.O.D.H.  Answer: Such  thing  originated  and  is  a  product  of  so-called Pentecost with the ―word of faith 
revival.‖ ―Word-Faith teachings trace their roots to E. W. Kenyon (1867-1948), a New England evangelical pastor 
who  taught  that  health  and  finances  were  the  right  of  every  believer  who  would  claim  the  promises  of Scripture 
through faith. Kenyon claimed God's promises  were realized by  our believing and verbally  confessing the relevant 
scriptures, and thus he coined the phrase, "What I confess, I possess." 
A more scriptural term for this is the ―get-rich‖ or ―prosperity‖ gospel. This  spirit  was  prophesied  to  be 
here in the end time. 
Revelation 3:17 <Because thou sayest, I am rich, and increased with goods, and have need of nothing.> 
Some  who  claim  to  be  Christians,  circled  their  neighbors  building,  vehicles  and  possibly  their  wives, 
claiming them in the name of the Lord.  
This is a lustful, greedy and covetous spirit, which has no regards for the following scriptures: 
Exodus 20:17 <Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's house, thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's wife, nor 
his manservant, nor his maidservant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour's.> 
I Timothy 6:5-11 <Perverse disputings of men of corrupt minds, and destitute of the truth, supposing that 
gain is godliness: from such withdraw thyself.  
But godliness with contentment is great gain.  
For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out.  
And having food and raiment let us be therewith content.  
But they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and [into] many foolish and hurtful lusts, which 
drown men in destruction and perdition.  
For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, 
and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.  
But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, 
meekness.> 
 
 
Question  # 22: ―There are ministers who, while they are preaching, act  as  though  they  are  under  the 
power of God. They pretendingly  cry, groan stammer and manifest other seemingly spiritual emotions. Were they 
really under the power of God to do those things?‖ 
 
E.O.D.H. Answer: No! This is a practice that is prevalent in so-called Pentecost and Bible school training 
that  message  ministers  have  adopted,  some  of  them  having  been  former  Pentecostals  preachers. This  traces  back 
even  further  than  Pentecostalism,  as  Jesus  found such  pretence amongst  the  religious  leaders  in  his  day. Matthew 
23:14 < Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye devour  widows' houses, and for a pretence make 
long prayer: therefore ye shall receive the greater damnation.> 
Men  who  are  not  genuinely  called  of  God  act  as  though  they  are  under  the  power  of  God.  This  is  gross 
hypocrisy and a form of godliness as these same men go out and commit adultery with women in the church. 
II Timothy 3:5 <Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.  
For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden  with sins, led away 
with divers lusts.> 

 
 
Question  # 23: ―Why  do  message  believers,  upon  greeting  one  another,  say, ―Hallelujah, praise the 
Lord, God bless etc. Are these things scriptural?  
 
E.O.D.H. Answer: We believe in saying all these things, as the scriptures support them. But it must not be 
done  as  a  form  or without  a true  meaning  from  the  heart. A  Christian  should  strive  to  say  with  a  true  meaning 
everything that comes out of his lips, and desist from all hypocrisies.  
Precious  brethren;  I  trust  that  this  subject:  ―Traits  of Pentecostalism  under  the  message‖,  will  be of 
edification  to  the  elected  Bride  internationally.  May  it  serve  to  lead,  guide  and  direct  true  message  believers  and 
ministers away from Pentecostalism and to desperately contend for the faith that was once delivered unto the saints; 
and even more desperately, to  contend for the final outpouring of the  Holy Spirit, which is soon to  come upon the 
end  time  Bride.  The  chapter  that  follows  is  inserted  in  this  book  to  give  all  our  readers  an honest  insight  into  the 
purpose of E.O.D.H., its authors, Bethel the House of God and all faith-like ministers around the world who believe, 
preach and contend for the promises of God to the end time Bride. Amen! 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Appointment of a minister &  
purpose of E.o.D.H. 
 
CHAPTER FOURTEEN 
 
 We  have  a  special  purpose  this  morning, and  that  is,  our  precious  Brother  Lloyd  Malama  will  be 
appointed, here at Bethel, to go back to Africa in the power of the Word and Spirit. So, it is a blessing, and we are 
more than happy to do this. After you heard his testimony, and having fellowshipped with Brother Malama, I know 
that you are all convinced that he is a true minister of the Lord and righteousness. So we will do that first, and then I 
will explain a little more as we go along.  
 So now, for this ordination I will read in the Book of Titus. Come, Brother Malama. Stand right here. I am 
reading from Titus 1: 4-11.  
 
<To Titus, mine own son after the common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and the 
Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.  
For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting, and ordain 
elders in every city, as I had appointed thee:  
If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of riot or unruly.  
For a bishop must be blameless, as the steward of God; not selfwilled, not soon angry, not given to wine, 
no striker, not given to filthy lucre (money);  
But a lover of hospitality, a lover of good men, sober, just, holy, temperate;  
Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort 
and to convince the gainsayers.  
For there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers, specially they of the circumcision:  
Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for (filthy 
money‘s sake) filthy lucre's sake.> (Titus 1: 4-11). 
 
May  the  Lord  add  a  blessing  to  this  portion  of  the  scripture.  And  in  line with our precious brother‘s 
appointment, I borrow the words of Brother Paul from II Timothy 4:1-8. 
 

<I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at 
his appearing and his kingdom;  
Preach  the  word;  be  instant  in  season,  out  of  season;  reprove,  rebuke,  exhort  with  all  longsuffering  and 
doctrine.  
For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap 
to themselves teachers, having itching ears;  
And they shall turn away their ears from the truth, and shall be turned unto fables.  
But  watch  thou  in  all  things,  endure  afflictions,  do  the  work  of  an  evangelist,  make  full  proof  of  thy 
ministry.  
For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand.  
I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith:  
Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give 
me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.> (II Timothy 4:1-8). 
 
May the Lord add a blessing to the reading of His Words. While we sing a couple verses, I will call Brother 
Barrow. I will call Brother Rogers as another witness. Come, brother. Come Brother Barrow. 
 
[Ed. Song leader and congregation sing, ―Speak my Lord‖, as the musicians play softly.] 
 
While  the  musicians  play  softly; we  give  our  precious  Brother  Malama  the right-hand of  fellowship,  in 
sanction  of  his  sincerity  and  honesty in  preaching  the  gospel  and  upholding  the  truth. We will  pray  in a moment. 
Brother Roy Barrow will join  hands  with  Brother  Malama,  giving  him  the  right-hand  of  fellowship with Brother 
Rogers Richardson. 
We  have  found  Brother  Malama  to  be  a  sincere  and  just  man,  set  for  the  defense of  the  gospel  of  Jesus 
Christ. Therefore, as the Lord appointed twelve and again appointed seventy; and as Brother Paul commanded Titus 
to ordain elders in every city, we follow the very same pattern in appointing our Brother Malama. Though he is from 
a  foreign  land (way  over  in  Zambia,  Africa) we  embrace  this  golden  opportunity  to  perform  this  blessing, upon 
Brother Lloyd Malama.  
We bow our heads in prayer. Brother Barrow and Brother Rogers will pray along with me.  
Yes,  Lord.  We  agree  together  that  we  have  found  the  right  spirit in  this  brother.  He  is  a  just and  upright 
man, as the prophet of God said on the book called,  ̳The Indictment‘ when he ordained the brothers. Lord, we come 
before  you  this  morning,  with  our  precious  Brother  Lloyd Malama,  giving him  the  right-hand  of  fellowship,  after 
charging him with the preaching of the gospel, even as Brother Paul did to Timothy.  
Lord, we  pray  that  you  will  freshly  anoint  Brother  Malama,  even  with  the anointing that  came  from  the 
seals.  Lord,  make  him  equipped. Make him  strong.  Make  him  bold.  Make  him  defiant.  According  to  the  Book  of 
Jude, let him contend for the faith that was once delivered unto the saints. Protect him from evil men. Protect him 
from dangers, even as You protected Brother Paul. We pray, Lord, that wherever he goes, he may preach the gospel 
and establish the truth, and that he may win many thousands of souls into your kingdom. 
We recognize the gift of  Brother Malama‘s, and Lord, we sanction that as elders here today, therefore we 
appoint Brother Malama as a preacher of righteousness, to go forth with the gospel into Africa and other parts of the 
world.  We  pray  above  all  that  when  the  outpouring  comes,  that  You  may  anoint  him,  Oh Lord,  with  a  fresh 
anointing,  with  the  fullness  of  the  Spirit,  and let him win  many  souls  into Your kingdom.  My  blessing upon  this 
brother and  may  it  abound, Oh Lord,  without  measure,  to  do  the  work  of  the  kingdom.  We  as elders of  Bethel 
sanction today, the calling of this brother and appoint him as a minister of righteousness, in the Name of Jesus Christ 
we  pray, with thanksgiving.  Amen. As  the  brothers  lifted  up  the hands  of  Moses,  we  also  lift  up  the hands  of  this 
brother, in the work of the ministry through Thy mighty name. 
We are happy this morning to witness this great deed, and we expect many fruits out of what was done here 
this morning. We may hear the news in the days to come of God using this brother to win thousands and thousands 
of souls in Africa. Yes.  
Because  of the Nicolaitan system that is existing around the world and under the message (trying to bind 
the servants of God) some in Africa were hesitant to ordain our brother, but we are happy to do this. And to defy that 
Nicolaitan system that wants him to come under their heresies, and under the heretics in order to sanction his calling 
and  sanction  the  gift  that  lays  in  his  bosom.  So  we  do  this  in  defiance to  all  that  oppose  him, and we  recognize 
Brother  Malama  as a minister  of  the  message  of  Malachi  4:  5 & 6, and  added  to  that,  as  a minister  of  end  time 
redemption that was loosed from under the seven seals. 

May  the  Lord  bless  and  prosper his  work  for the  ministry, and  may  the  blessing  of  the  Lord  be  upon 
Brother Malama that he may win many souls into the kingdom of God. May his ministry reach throughout the world 
that  we  do  not  even  hope  to  reach  in  the  near  future. That was  one  of  the  reasons  for  our precious brother  being 
ordained here at Bethel. The enemy cannot keep down a servant of God. No. 
 
 
Appointment by apostolic pattern 
 
Brother Malama, this day, was appointed by apostolic pattern and he will straightway preach to us, even as 
the Bible said, ―And Paul straightway declared the gospel in Damascus.‖ He will also straightway declare the gospel 
at midday today, after my exhortation. 
So, our precious brother was appointed according to apostolic pattern. If you notice that in Matthew 10 and 
Luke 10, it  spake nothing about  laying  on  of  hands  of  our Lord  upon them.  He  just  said  that  they  were  appointed. 
And  when  Paul  gave  Timothy and Titus the  command, he never  said, ―Lay hands upon them,‖ but all that he said 
was, ―Ordain elders, even as I have appointed you.‖ 
Nothing is wrong with laying on of hands. The Bible said, ―By the laying on of the hands of the presbytery, 
stir up the gift that is in thee.‖ But that is an old Jewish custom. The prophet of God said that we should be far past 
the  days  of  ̳laying  on  of  hands,‘ and be like the gentiles. Yes. ―Speak the word only and  my  servant  shall  be 
healed.‖ 
So, I make this declaration, not only for Brother Malama today, but I make it for the whole House of God 
here, all under the sound of my voice and wherever this message may go, that people will understand the functions 
of Bethel the House of God and also E.O.D.H., the publishing arm of Bethel the House of God. 
We were tremendously blessed by the testimony of Brother Malama. It gave us an insight into how far and 
deep into the world and the rural areas that E.O.D.H. has gone. It has gone far and wide. 
Brother Malama is appointed as a minister of the message of Malachi 4 and end time redemption that was 
loosed from under the seals. He represents the message of  Brother Branham. Not a cult or not an ism of any kind. 
And people who consider Bethel and E.O.D.H. a cult and a new movement are wrong, therefore, he is not ordained 
as a minister of E.O.D.H. That is just a little arm of the message. He is not ordained as a minister of Bethel. That is 
only a little arm of the message of Malachi 4. But he is ordained as a minister of the message of Malachi 4: 5 & 6, in 
its  fullness, and in  its  purity.  And  above  all,  he  is  ordained  as a  minister  of  end  time  redemption,  which  we  also 
uphold, and which we also preach. How many agree with these things? [Congregation says, ―Amen!‖]  
We have recognized a call in the brother‘s life, and we have sanctioned the same. We have appointed him

Article #14: We, with all our affiliate churches internationally, are obliged to practice Bible standards of the Ten

God (Exodus chapter 20: 7-17). 
 Adventist people keep Saturday holy, but  we keep seven days holy, because  we live a sanctified and holy 
life every day, and we rest from sin, and that is God‘s call in the New Testament, ―Come unto me, all ye that labour, 
and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest, and ye shall find rest unto your souls.‖ So, our souls are resting from 
sin.  We  are  the  true  Sabbatharians,  but  not  Saturdarians.  The  world  is  filled  and  Tobago  is  filled  with  a  lot  of 
Saturdarians, but we are the true Sabbatharians, because our souls are resting from sin.

GOD BEING PERFECT, HE MUST BE PERFECT IN HIS DECISIONS

 Now, if you'll be real understanding, or try to be, you'll see why I have took always the stand for the Word

alone.
